#+TITLE: Record & Grade — Music Performance Assessment
#+AUTHOR: Sean Coleman
#+STARTUP: overview
#+OPTIONS: toc:2

* Goal
Create assessments delivered through a website where students record themselves
*playing / singing / entering MIDI*, and the system *automatically grades* the
performance. Also save raw recordings for *manual review*.

Target tasks (kept deliberately simple):
- *Pitch-blind rhythmic accuracy* — grade timing only, ignore pitch.
- *Rhythm-blind tonal accuracy* — grade pitch only, ignore timing.
- Very short passages: *1 to 5 pitches*.

* Integrated into pages-dev (2026-06-22)
This project was moved into the *pages-dev* music platform as a Quarto app:
~quarto-demo/apps/assessment/~ (this file lives there now).

** What was wired up
- App: ~quarto-demo/apps/assessment/index.html~ — the tonal-accuracy grader,
  generalized from a fixed 3-note demo to an *N-note* grader (N = number of
  notes in the chosen pattern).
- Targets now come from the existing *ABC pattern library*
  (~../patterns/pattern-object.js~) instead of manual dropdowns:
  - Collection dropdown = keys of ~patternObject~ (major-stepwise, minor-stepwise,
    major-I-V, minor-i-V).
  - Pattern dropdown = tunes within the collection (parsed by ~ABCJS.TuneBook~).
  - Notation rendered with the vendored abcjs (~../shared/abcjs-basic.js~);
    solfège pulled from the ~w:~ line.
- *Grading targets* (MIDI numbers for the V1 melody) are parsed by a small
  purpose-built module: ~abc-pitch.js~ (~AbcPitch.melodyMidi(abc)~).
  - Why a custom parser and not abcjs's MIDI: it's deterministic and *testable
    in node* (verified C major→Do-Re-Mi, C minor flattens E/A/B, explicit ~=B~
    naturals, chord-symbol skipping). abcjs is still used for notation + the
    library parse.
  - Scope/limitation: handles the constrained ABC in pattern-object.js (keys
    C/Cm, single notes, octave ,/' marks, ^ _ = accidentals, trailing durations,
    "w:" lyrics). Per-note accidentals only (no measure-persistence) — exact for
    this data; revisit if richer ABC is added.
- Kept from the standalone demo: YIN detection, damped needle, silence-gap
  segmentation, octave-tolerance toggle (default on), MediaRecorder save
  (filename now embeds collection + pattern id + grade).
- Site wiring: ~practice/assessment.qmd~ embeds the app via iframe and is added
  to the Practice navbar menu in ~_quarto.yml~. ~apps/**~ is already in Quarto
  ~resources~, so the app ships as-is.

** Verified
- ~abc-pitch.js~ parser output checked in node against real pattern data.
- App JS passes ~node --check~.
- All resources resolve over HTTP (index, abc-pitch.js, shared/abcjs-basic.js,
  patterns/pattern-object.js → 200).

** [2026-06-22] Response notation
- After recording, the detected pitches are rendered as notation via abcjs in the
  same style as the pattern (stemless "0" noteheads, light panel, %%score (V1)).
- ~midiToAbcToken(midi, minorKey)~ inverts MIDI→ABC, spelling notes under the
  pattern's key: diatonic notes rely on the key signature, exceptions get an
  explicit accidental (e.g. Cm: sung Eb → "E", sung B-natural → "=B"; major: Eb →
  "_E"). Verified in node across 12 cases.
- Shows for any non-empty detection (even when the note count ≠ expected), so the
  student sees what was actually heard. ~#responseCard~ / ~#responseNotation~.

** [2026-06-22] Notation polish + controls + establish tonality
- *Fully black notation*: abcjs inherits ~currentColor~, which was the page's
  light dark-mode text → forced ~color:#000~ on the staves + ~fill:#000~ on the
  pattern SVG.
- *Disabled click-to-color*: all ~renderAbc~ calls now pass ~selectTypes:false~
  (and ~cursor:default~), so clicking notes no longer selects/colors them.
- *Response coloring*: response renders with ~add_classes:true~; ~colorNotes()~
  walks ~.abcjs-note~ in order and sets fill green (#138000) for in-tolerance
  notes, red (#cc0000) for out (index-aligned to targets; extra notes red).
- *Octave folding + 8va*: each sung note is folded into the displayed octave
  (kept on the staff) and gets a text marker (8va/8vb/15ma/15mb, or "+N8ve") when
  it was actually sung that many octaves from the target. Implemented by
  rendering ~written = sung - 12*octShift - transpose~ then ~visualTranspose~,
  so spelling/key/clef match the pattern automatically.
- *Key / Octave / Clef controls*: ~transpose = keyOffset + 12*octave~ is applied
  to both the targets (what to sing) and the display (~visualTranspose~); clef is
  display-only (appended to the K: line). Changing any re-renders + re-targets;
  a shown response re-renders too (~lastDetected~).
- *Establish tonality* button: plays I–IV–V7–I (triangle tones) in the current
  key. *Major only* for now (minor/other tonalities deferred — shows a note).

** [2026-06-23] Rhythm assessment app (pitch-blind rhythmic accuracy)
- New app ~quarto-demo/apps/rhythm-assessment/~ — the other half of the goal.
  Reuses the tonal app's card/shell design; swaps the engine for timing.
- Targets from ~../rhythms/rhythm-pattern-object.js~ (collection + pattern
  dropdowns via ~ABCJS.TuneBook~; notation via vendored abcjs, fully black).
- ~abc-rhythm.js~ (~AbcRhythm.parse(abc)~) → ~{events, onsets, total, lQuarters}~
  in L-unit beats. Handles durations (B2/B3/B/ etc.), broken rhythm (>,<),
  rests (z, advance no onset), ties (- → continuation, no new onset).
  *Verified in node*: 5 spot-checks (incl. broken/rest/tie) + full-file sweep
  (every pattern parses to valid onsets).
- Detection: *energy-based onset detection* (not pitch). Per-frame RMS captured
  during the window; post-processed rising-edge peak picking with refractory
  (~110ms). Live input-level meter + live attack counter; "Onset sensitivity"
  control (threshold).
- Grading is *tempo-LOCKED* (changed 2026-06-23 per request — was tempo-
  independent): the count-in sets the tempo and the performance is graded in
  *absolute seconds* against it.
  - Tempo slider = *macrobeats per minute*. ~secPerLunit = (60/bpm)/macroLUnits~.
  - ~macroLUnits~ (macrobeat length in L-units) is meter-derived:
    ~AbcRhythm.macrobeatLUnits(abc, isTriple)~ = (isTriple?3:1)*(4/d)/lQuarters;
    ~isTriple~ from the collection name. *Verified in node*: every pattern is a
    whole number of macrobeats (all 4).
  - Target attack times (sec) = onset_Lunits * secPerLunit, relative to the
    downbeat. Tolerance is in *ms* (default ±100). Requires matching attack count.
- *Count-in synchronized with capture*: on Record, N macrobeat clicks (N=2 or 4,
  selectable) are scheduled on ~audioCtx~; the downbeat after them is mapped into
  ~performance.now()~ via a one-shot ~(ctxNow, perfNow)~ offset → ~perfT0~.
  Detected onsets are measured as seconds from ~perfT0~. Capture window auto-sized
  = count-in + pattern duration + 700ms tail. UI flips "Count-in…" → "Perform
  now!" at the downbeat; live attack counter only tallies after ~perfT0~ (so
  count-in click leakage isn't counted).
- Feedback: per-attack table (target s / your s / off ms / hit) + overall
  PASS/FAIL + an *absolute-time timeline* (target row vs your row, green/red).
- Playback: "Hear pattern (with count-in)" = count-in then a model performance
  (square-wave clicks) at the chosen tempo.
- Caveat: count-in clicks play through speakers and can leak into the mic;
  they're before ~perfT0~ so filtered out, but *headphones recommended*. Onset
  resolution still ~rAF-bound (~16ms).

** [2026-06-23] Metronome + latency compensation (lag fix)
- User reported perceptible lag making it hard. Two fixes:
  1. *Latency compensation*: ~latencyComp() = audioCtx.outputLatency ||
     baseLatency~. Clicks are HEARD ~outputLatency after being scheduled, so
     grading-from-scheduled-time made every attack read late. ~perfT0~ now adds
     ~lat~ (= heard downbeat). Residual = mic input latency (not exposed by the
     API) — could add a manual calibration offset later.
  2. *Continuous metronome* (count-in + through the whole performance), via
     ~scheduleMetronome(ctxNow, perfNow, nBeats, downbeatIndex, spm, lat)~:
     - *Audible* click on each macrobeat (toggle ~#metroAudible~, default on;
       labeled "headphones" since it leaks into the mic otherwise). Accent on
       beat 0 and the downbeat.
     - *Visual* crisp flash on a beat dot (~#beatdot~, toggle ~#metroVisual~,
       default on). Flash timing offset by ~lat~ so it aligns with the heard
       click; ~setTimeout~ list cleared via ~clearVisualTimers()~ on stop/restart.
- Same metronome now also drives "Hear pattern" (count-in + steady macrobeat +
  model attacks).
- Removed old ~scheduleCountIn~ (superseded by ~scheduleMetronome~).
- User confirmed audible metronome works well even without headphones.

** [2026-06-23] Performed-rhythm notation (generated ABC)
- Now feasible because grading is tempo-LOCKED: detected onset seconds → L-units
  (÷secPerLunit) → quantize to nearest half-beat (~GRID=0.5~) → durations from
  inter-onset deltas → ABC. (This was unreliable in the tempo-independent version
  and was explicitly deferred then.)
- ~performedAbc(detSec)~ reuses the target's M:/L: and ~K: clef=none~, inserts
  barlines at measure boundaries (~measureLUnits = 4n/(d·lQuarters)~), last note
  fills to ~target.total~. ~abcDur()~ renders half-beat durations (e.g. 1.5 →
  "3/2", 0.5 → "/2"). *Verified in node*: perfect performances reproduce the
  target rhythm (duple/triple/broken).
- ~renderPerformed()~ + ~colorPerformed()~: green (#138000)/red (#cc0000)
  noteheads per attack when counts match (analog to the tonal app's response);
  uncolored when counts mismatch (still shown, so the student sees what they did).
  New card ~#performedCard~ / ~#performedNotation~.
- Barlines + beams: ABC beams via whitespace (adjacent short notes w/o spaces
  beam; spaces break beams). ~performedAbc~ now emits beam groups by writing
  notes <a quarter (~dur·lQuarters<1~) without spaces, breaking at macrobeat
  boundaries and barlines; barlines at ~measureLUnits~. *Verified in node*:
  duple/triple/duple-division reproduce the source beaming + barlines exactly.
- MediaRecorder save (filename embeds collection + pattern id + grade), same as
  tonal app.
- Site wiring: ~practice/assessment-rhythm.qmd~ (iframe) + navbar entry in
  ~_quarto.yml~.
- Design deltas vs tonal app (intentional): no key/octave/clef or establish-
  tonality (pitch concepts); response shown as a timeline rather than re-notated
  (quantizing free onsets back to notation is unreliable — timeline is honest).

** [2026-06-23] Spacebar flow + mic gating (rhythm app)
- Record button stays disabled until mic enabled (was already; now also enforced
  by the flow + a status banner).
- Spacebar state machine (~flowState~, banner ~#flowStatus~):
  nomic → (enable mic) ready → SPACE hear → listened → SPACE record → recording
  → pass: "passed" (SPACE = next pattern) | fail/incomplete: "failed" (SPACE =
  re-record). ~advanceNext()~ steps pattern → next collection → wrap; ~loadPattern~
  sets flow ready (or nomic). Buttons (Hear/Record) call the same
  ~hearPattern()~ / ~recordAttempt()~ so clicks and SPACE stay in sync.
- Keydown handler ignores SPACE when focus is on INPUT/SELECT (so sliders/selects
  keep native space behavior); else preventDefault (no page scroll / no double
  button activation). Note: in the Quarto iframe the user must click the app once
  to give it focus before SPACE works.
- Marked as the *starting* flow; to be extended later (per user).

** [2026-06-23] Constant mode (automatic call-and-response loop)
- "∞ Constant mode" button (enabled with mic). Runs hands-free:
  per cycle = *model plays automatically* (no count-in) → ~CONST_GAP=2~ macrobeats
  of clicks ("your turn") → *record* → grade → pass: ~advanceNext()~, fail: same
  pattern → ~setTimeout(runConstantCycle, 500)~ loop.
- Metronome is *constant*: one ~scheduleMetronome~ call spans the whole cycle
  (model + gap + record), accenting both the model downbeat (0) and the record
  downbeat (~pm+CONST_GAP~). ~perfT0~ = record downbeat (model/gap onsets are
  negative → filtered out of grading).
- ~scheduleMetronome~ generalized: ~accents~ is now an array of beat indices
  (was a single ~downbeatIndex~); the two manual call sites pass ~[0, N]~.
- ~capturing~ banner shows phases Listen… → Your turn… → Perform!.
- Stop: the Constant button toggles, or *SPACE bails out* (~stopConstant()~).
  Caveat: already-scheduled audio clicks for the in-flight cycle finish playing
  after Stop (oscillators aren't tracked for cancellation); it won't loop again.
- Manual Record/Hear buttons disabled while constant mode runs.

** [2026-06-23] Constant mode → Continuous mode (refined)
- Renamed constant→continuous (button id ~#continuous~, vars/functions).
- New cycle timing: model plays *with* metronome (beats 0..pm-1) → *NO clicks*,
  straight into a *silent recording* (beats pm..2pm-1; record downbeat = right
  after the model, no pre-record gap) → grade → *2 turnaround clicks* (beats 2pm,
  2pm+1) → pass: ~advanceNext()~, fail: same pattern → loop. The 2 clicks moved
  from before-record to after-record.
  - Bonus: silent recording window means zero click bleed during grading.
- ~scheduleMetronome~ refactored into per-beat ~scheduleBeat()~ (continuous mode
  schedules non-contiguous beats: model + turnaround, skipping the record window).
- Status bar (~#flowStatus~ via new ~setStatus(text,cls)~) now drives continuous
  feedback: "🔊 Listen…" → "🎤 Your turn — perform!" → "✓ Passed — next pattern"
  / "✗ Not yet — repeating this pattern" (visible through the turnaround).
- Performed-rhythm notation moved to the *top of the Result card* and now
  *persists until the next recording starts* (hidden at the record downbeat, not
  on pattern load/advance). ~loadPattern~ no longer hides it and skips the flow
  reset while continuous mode runs.
- Tweaks: metronome clicks ON through the recording again (model+record = beats
  0..2pm-1 via one ~scheduleMetronome~, accents at 0 and pm); model→record still
  immediate (no gap); the 2-beat turnaround is now *silent* (no clicks). Feedback
  shown ASAP: grade()+status fire synchronously at ~perfEndMs+150ms~, then the
  recording playback card is presented after the async blob resolves.

** [2026-06-23] Steady layout (no wiggle) + collapsible options (rhythm app)
- Wiggle cause: result elements toggled ~display:none/block~ → layout reflow.
  Fix: keep them in the layout always; ~.hidden{visibility:hidden}~ + reserved CSS
  heights (~#performedNotation~ 64, ~#captureSlot~ 24, ~#resultArea~ 150,
  ~#recCard~ 108). Helpers ~show(id)/hide(id)~ toggle the class.
  - performedCard: hidden via class (reserved), shown after grade.
  - timelineWrap + recCard: now *always visible* (empty timeline + "No recording
    yet." placeholders); ~clearTimeline()~ / ~resetRecCard()~ manage content.
- Moved secondary options to a bottom ~<details id="options">~ ("Options &
  diagnostics"): timing tolerance, count-in, onset sensitivity, metronome
  toggles, and the live input-level meter. Kept up top: collection/pattern/
  next/random, notation, tempo, Hear, mic/record/continuous, beat dot, status.
- Note: result table can still exceed the 150px reserve for high-onset patterns
  (minor growth); cards no longer appear/disappear, which was the big jump.

** [2026-06-23] Acoustic latency calibration (speakers)
- "🎯 Calibrate latency (speakers)" button in Options. ~calibrateLatency()~
  plays 6 loud short clicks (0.4s apart), captures mic frames, and for each
  click finds the detected onset within (sched, sched+350ms); the *median* of
  (detected − scheduled) = full round-trip latency (output + propagation + mic
  input). Stored in ~calibratedLatencyMs~.
- ~latencyComp()~ now returns the calibrated round trip when available, else
  falls back to ~audioCtx.outputLatency~ (which omits unmeasurable mic input
  latency). This feeds ~perfT0~ everywhere, removing the residual lag for
  speaker users.
- Works *only on speakers* (mic must hear the clicks) and relies on
  ~echoCancellation:false~ (already set). Precision is rAF-bound (~16ms); median
  of 6 mitigates. Upgrade path: sample-accurate cross-correlation vs the known
  click waveform (AudioWorklet/OfflineAudioContext) for ~1ms.

** [2026-06-23] Ported rhythm-app UX to the tonal app
- Brought the workflow/UX refinements from rhythm-assessment to ~apps/assessment~:
  - *Status bar* (~#flowStatus~) + ~setStatus/setFlow~.
  - *Spacebar flow*: ready → SPACE hear → record → pass: next / fail: re-record.
    Buttons (Hear/Record) call shared ~hearPattern()~ / ~recordAttempt()~.
  - *Continuous mode* (auto loop): model plays target pitches (with gaps) →
    record window (capture dur) → grade → pass: ~advanceNext()~, fail: repeat →
    loop. Uses ~flowTimers~/~clearFlowTimers~. *No metronome/count-in/tempo/latency*
    — tonal is rhythm-blind (fixed-duration capture, silence segmentation).
  - *Steady layout*: ~.hidden~ (visibility) + reserved heights (#responseNotation,
    #captureSlot, #resultArea, #recCard); ~show/hide~ helpers. responseCard moved
    to *top of Result card* and persists until the next recording; recCard always
    visible with "No recording yet." placeholder (~resetRecCard~).
  - *Collapsible bottom options*: tolerance, capture duration, octave toggle moved
    into ~<details id="options">~. Kept up top: collection/pattern/next/random,
    key/octave/clef, notation/solfège, Establish/Hear, mic/record/continuous, Live.
  - Continuous button mic-gated; ~<kbd>SPACE</kbd>~ hint in intro.
- *Intentionally NOT ported* (timing-specific, N/A to rhythm-blind tonal):
  metronome (audible/visual), count-in, tempo, acoustic latency calibration.

** [2026-06-23] Time-per-note capture (tonal app)
- Replaced the fixed "Capture (s)" selector with *"Time per note (s)"* (#perNote,
  default 0.8; options 0.6/0.8/1.0/1.5). Capture window now scales with the
  pattern: ~captureMs() = (targets.length * perNoteSec() + CAPTURE_TAIL[0.6]) * 1000~.
- ~hearPattern~, ~recordAttempt~, and ~runContinuousCycle~ all use ~perNoteSec()~:
  model note spacing = perNote (sound = 0.7·perNote), capture = captureMs(). Fixes
  awkward fixed windows when patterns have different note counts (1–5).

** [2026-06-23] Download full continuous session (responses only) — tonal app
- New ~#sessionCard~ with a "⬇ Download responses" link. One audio file containing
  *only the student responses* from a continuous session (model playback + gaps
  excluded).
- Implemented via a session-level ~MediaRecorder~ (~sessionRecorder~) started+paused
  when Continuous mode begins; ~sessionResume()~ at each response-window start,
  ~sessionPause()~ + ~sessionResponses++~ at grade. ~finalizeSession()~ on Stop builds
  one Blob from the buffered chunks → ~session_<ts>_<N>responses.<ext>~.
  - pause/resume yields a single valid container with only the recorded spans;
    guarded with try/catch (pause/resume unsupported → degrades, not fatal).
  - Runs alongside the existing per-cycle recorder (last-attempt playback); two
    MediaRecorders on one micStream.
** [2026-06-23] Session refinements (both apps)
- *SRT export* alongside the session audio: ~buildSRT(sessionEntries)~ where each
  entry = ~{label: "<collection> — Pattern <id>", durMs}~ logged at grade time;
  timestamps accumulate over the responses-only audio so they line up. Second
  link "⬇ Download labels (SRT)" in ~#sessionCard~. Verified format in node.
- *Immediate STOP*: ~stopAllTones()~ tracks scheduled oscillators (~activeOscs~ in
  ~playTone~/rhythm ~click~) and stops them at once; ~endSession()~ also clears the
  cycle timers, stops the per-cycle MediaRecorder, finalizes the session, and
  hides capture UI — no more waiting for audio/recording to play out.
- *End at collection end*: passing the last pattern in the collection →
  ~endSession("complete")~ with a "🎉 Session complete" status (does not wrap to
  the next collection in continuous mode).

*** Tonal-only
- *Establish tonality* once per session at start: I–IV–V7–I (major) / i–iv–V7–i
  (minor), then the first cycle. ~establishTonality()~ now supports minor and the
  standalone button works for both.
- *Time per note*: removed the user selector; fixed ~PLAY_PER_NOTE=0.6~ (playback)
  and ~REC_PER_NOTE=0.8~ (recording window = N·0.8 + tail).

*** Rhythm app — transferable parts applied
- Added the session recorder + SRT, ~stopAllTones~/immediate STOP, and end-at-
  collection. NOT applied (N/A to pitch-blind rhythm): establish tonality, the
  0.6/0.8 per-note timing (rhythm is tempo/metronome-driven).

** [2026-06-23] Hybrid segmentation — pitch-change + smaller gaps (tonal app)
- Notes now split on a *sustained pitch change* as well as silence, so legato
  singing with distinct pitches no longer needs gaps. Gap threshold reduced
  ~GAP_MS 120→70~, ~MIN_VOICED 5→4~.
- ~segmentNotes()~: split decisions run on an *EMA-smoothed* contour (~ALPHA_SEG
  0.3~) so vibrato/wobble don't false-split, while the note pitch is the median
  of RAW frames. New note when a candidate holds ~NEW_NOTE_FRAMES~ steady frames
  ≥ ~NEW_NOTE_CENTS(80)~ from the current note's smoothed ref (~SAME_CENTS 70~ =
  same-note tolerance). Glide frames auto-discarded (candidate resets while
  unstable).
- *Verified in node*: legato whole-steps → 3 notes, legato semitone → 2, wide
  vibrato ±55¢ → 1 note, descending leap → 2, same-pitch-with-gap → 2.
- *Limitation*: repeated SAME-pitch notes still need a small gap (no pitch change
  to detect; would need energy/onset detection to split re-articulations). Intro
  copy updated to say so.

** [2026-06-23] Segmentation #3 — offline optimal N-segment partition (tonal)
- Replaced the streaming EMA hybrid (too twitchy w/ vibrato/glide) with an
  *offline DP*: grading is batch + N is known, so partition the captured pitch
  contour into *exactly N* contiguous segments minimizing within-segment variance.
  Boundaries land at real transitions; vibrato/wobble = within-segment spread,
  glides too short to form a segment → not twitchy.
- ~dpPartition(x, mand, N)~: O(N·T²) DP over MIDI-float series with prefix sums
  for variance·n; ~mand[i]~ = mandatory boundary at a silent gap (segments can't
  span it). ~segment()~ groups voiced frames into gap-delimited chunks (drop
  <MIN_VOICED), then: chunks>N → count mismatch (return chunk medians); else DP
  into N; each segment's pitch = median of RAW freqs.
- Gaps still force boundaries → same-pitch repeats separate when a gap is left;
  over-singing (more chunks than N) → "Detected X, expected N".
- *Verified in node*: legato C-D-E →3; legato semitone →2; wide vibrato ±55¢ →1;
  C-D-E each w/ wide vibrato →3; descending leap →2; same-pitch+gap →2; flat sing
  vs C-D-E →C C C (fails notes 2-3 correctly).
- Tradeoff (accepted): under-singing *with chunks ≤ N* is force-split to N rather
  than flagged incomplete, but per-note cents grading still fails the missing
  note. Same-pitch repeats without a gap remain unsplittable (pitch-only).

** [2026-06-23] Shuffle pattern order (both apps)
- "🔀 Shuffle order" checkbox (next to Next/Random). A ~playOrder~ array defines
  traversal within the current collection; ~buildOrder()~ (Fisher–Yates when
  checked) runs on collection load and on toggle. Next, spacebar advance, and
  continuous-mode advance all step through ~playOrder~ via ~gotoOrder/orderPos~;
  ~isLastInCollection()~ (order-based) ends a continuous session after the last
  pattern in the shuffled order. Collection load starts at ~playOrder[0]~.
- Independent of continuous mode (works for manual Next too). The "Random" button
  (one-off jump) is unchanged.
- Applied to both the tonal and rhythm apps.

** [2026-06-23] Assessment-type dropdown (tonal app)
- New ~#assess~ dropdown: "Repeat the pattern" (default), "Sing the resting tone",
  "Sing the first pitch". Tonal-only (pitch concepts; N/A to rhythm app).
- The *stimulus* (notation shown + "Hear pattern"/continuous model) is always the
  full pattern; only the *graded target* changes via ~gradeTargets()~:
  repeat → all ~targets~; resting → ~[60+transpose]~ (tonic in current key/octave);
  first → ~[targets[0]]~.
- Wired ~gradeTargets()~ through ~captureMs~ (window sized to graded length),
  ~segment()~ N, ~grade()~, ~renderResponse()~, and ~targetInfo~ ("Sing the …").
  Change listener on #assess re-applies. Recording filename + SRT label now
  include the assess type for review.

** [2026-06-23] Combined tune assessment — AUDIO PROTOTYPE
- New app ~quarto-demo/apps/tune-assessment/~ — grades real tunes on *both* pitch
  and rhythm from one monophonic audio capture. Enabled by the offline DP
  pitch-segmentation insight (F0 segmentation is robust; onsets give timing).
- ~abc-tune.js~ (~AbcTune.melodyNotes(abc)~): full ABC melody parser for the tunes
  library — key signatures (any major/minor/mode via circle-of-fifths),
  per-measure accidental persistence, octave marks, durations (incl broken
  rhythm > <), rests, ties, chord-symbol skipping. Returns
  ~{notes:[{midi,onset,dur}], total, lq, meter, tempoQ}~. *Verified in node*:
  Lightly Row (Bb)=F D D Eb C C Bb…, Yankee Doodle (Eb), Gm tune w/ ^F accidental;
  all 109 tunes parse cleanly.
- Grading (combined): tempo-locked (count-in + quarter metronome, tune's Q,
  tempo slider). YIN F0 + energy per frame. ~detectOnsets~ (energy) → performed
  onset times; *greedy alignment* of each target note to the nearest unused
  onset within a window → rhythm deviation (ms); pitch = median F0 in that note's
  window vs target (cents, octave-tolerant). Per-note table (pitch/timing ✓),
  scores "Pitch x/N · Rhythm y/N · Both z/N", + rhythm timeline.
- Reuses: YIN, energy onset, metronome+latencyComp, MediaRecorder save, steady-ish
  layout, status bar. Wired via ~practice/assessment-tune.qmd~ + navbar.
- *Known limits (honest)*: monophonic + clear attacks only; legato & repeated
  same-pitch notes = missed onsets (greedy align tolerates, shows as misses);
  onset/pitch resolution rAF-bound (~16ms) so slow the tempo for fast tunes;
  no continuous/session/SRT yet (prototype). MIDI path still the reliable option.
- [2026-06-23] Added *chord accompaniment* (play-along): ~abc-tune.js~ now parses
  chord symbols → ~chords:[{onset,midis,name}]~ via ~chordMidis()~ (root+quality →
  triad/7th in the C3 region). ~scheduleChords()~ plays soft triangle pads
  synced to the metronome/tempo in both Hear-tune and record. Toggle ~#chords~
  (default on). *Requires headphones* — accompaniment through speakers bleeds into
  the mic and corrupts onset/pitch detection; toggle off if no headphones.

** [2026-06-23] Class session logging + makeup replay (all 3 apps, local-first)
- Shared ~apps/shared/session-log.js~: floating "Class log" panel. Teacher
  improvises → each presented item logged (deduped) in order; *Export* JSON or
  *Copy link* (state base64-encoded in URL ~#makeup=…~). Student opens that link →
  app enters *replay/makeup* mode and steps the exact sequence (Next/Prev).
- Generic module; each app supplies ~getState()~ (snapshot + ~label~) and
  ~applyState(s)~ (set controls for replay). Calls: ~SessionLog.init({...})~,
  ~SessionLog.present()~ on each load/param change, ~SessionLog.begin()~ after init.
  - tonal: logs coll/idx/key/octave/clef/assess (present() in applyControls).
  - rhythm: coll/idx/tempo/countin (present() in loadPattern + tempo/countin).
  - tune: coll/idx/tempo/chords (present() in loadTune + tempo/chords).
- Event shape is flat (~{app, occurred_at, ...state}~) → maps onto Supabase
  ~session_events~ later (architecture.org Phase 1/2: log → makeup pages).
- *Verified in node*: unicode-safe link encode/decode roundtrip.
- Local-first prototype: in-memory + URL/JSON; no backend yet. NEXT: POST events
  to Supabase + persist recordings; per-student log; unify cross-app session link.
- [2026-06-23] *Auto-advance replay on pass*: ~SessionLog.onResult(pass)~ — in
  replay mode a pass advances to the next step after 1.5s (result stays visible
  first); last step → "✅ Makeup complete". Manual Prev/Next still work.
  Pass source: tonal/rhythm ~summary==="pass"~ (in recordAttempt completion);
  tune = every note correct on both axes (~bothPass===N~, in grade()).

** [2026-06-23] De-iframe → inline page integration (tonal = proof)
- Goal: no independent scroll + inherit site CSS → requires removing the iframe
  (iframe = separate doc = own scrollbar + isolated CSS). Decision: keep dark
  component styling, scoped; tonal first.
- Verified ~{{< include >}}~ passes raw HTML/CSS/JS through intact (throwaway test).
- Converted ~apps/assessment/index.html~ into a *scoped fragment* (single source,
  works standalone AND embedded): dropped <!DOCTYPE>/<html>/<head>/<body>; all CSS
  nested under ~#app-root~ (native CSS nesting); ~#app-root~ carries the dark bg +
  max-width container; *dropped font-family* so it inherits the site font; script
  srcs → absolute ~/apps/...~; body wrapped in ~<div id="app-root">~.
- ~practice/assessment.qmd~: replaced the iframe with
  ~::: {.column-page}\n{{< include ../apps/assessment/index.html >}}\n:::~.
- Verified: rendered page has #app-root inline, 0 iframes, abcjs ref present;
  standalone + in-site both serve 200.
- Caveat: relies on native CSS nesting (modern browsers); standalone fragment has
  no <head> charset/viewport (fine on localhost/desktop).
- *Fix (first attempt failed → black-on-black raw HTML)*: ~{{< include >}}~ runs
  the file through pandoc as Markdown, so blank lines in the big inline <script>
  (and indented markup) became code blocks / escaped text. Working recipe:
  1. Style → ~apps/assessment/app.css~ (scoped under #app-root, nested).
  2. Inline script → ~apps/assessment/app.js~ (860 lines).
  3. Markup → a *raw-block partial* ~practice/_assessment-embed.qmd~ wrapping
     ~<link app.css>~ + ~<div id="app-root">…</div>~ + script srcs in a
     ` ```{=html} ` block (guaranteed verbatim passthrough).
  4. ~practice/assessment.qmd~: ~::: {.column-page}\n{{< include _assessment-embed.qmd >}}\n:::~.
  5. Removed the old standalone ~index.html~ (partial is canonical; app.css/app.js/
     abc-pitch.js are shared resources, copied to _site/apps on render).
  - Verified: 0 escaped tags / 0 <pre> in output; #app-root + controls present;
    app.css/app.js linked + copied to _site; partial (~_~ prefix) not a standalone page.
  - Lost: direct ~/apps/<app>/index.html~ view (use the rendered site / preview).
- [2026-06-23] *Rolled out to rhythm + tune* (scripted transform): each now has
  ~app.css~ (scoped #app-root) + ~app.js~ + ~practice/_rhythm-embed.qmd~ /
  ~_tune-embed.qmd~ (raw-block partial); ~assessment-rhythm.qmd~ /
  ~assessment-tune.qmd~ include them in ~.column-page~; index.html removed for
  both. Verified: 0 leakage, #app-root + css present, app.css/app.js reachable.
  All three assessment tools are now inline-in-site (no iframe, no independent

** [2026-06-23] Tune makeup = engagement bar (not mastery)
- Tune pass-off is now mode-dependent: normal use stays strict (~bothPass===N~);
  *makeup/replay* (~SessionLog.isReplay()~) passes on *evidence of engagement* —
  ~perf.length >= max(2, ceil(N*0.4))~ OR ~bothPass >= max(1, ceil(N*0.25))~ (a
  real attempt, given the janky audio detection). Result line notes "(makeup:
  counts if you gave it a real go)".
- Tunable knobs in ~apps/tune-assessment/app.js~ grade(). Tonal/rhythm makeup
  still use their binary pass (could get a similar engagement bar if wanted).

** [2026-06-24] Shared tonality (DO / resting tone persists across tools)
- ~apps/shared/tonality.js~: one "current tonic" as pitch class 0–11 (C=0) in
  localStorage ("musicTonic"), synced across tabs (storage event). ~bindSelect(el,
  "pc"|"note")~ sets a tool's tonic control from the shared value on load (firing
  the tool's own change handler) and writes back on user change. ~parseNote~
  handles pc indices, note names + enharmonics ("A#/Bb", "Db", "G major", "Eb4").
  Verified parse + get/set in node.
- Wired (confident — both are pitch-class, C=0): *solfège #do-selector* ("DO") and
  *tonal assessment #key* ("resting tone" = tonic). These are exactly what the
  user named; switching one persists to the other on navigation.
- Wired (best-effort note-name; defensive no-op if it can't parse): chord-library
  *scales #root-select*, *chord-sheet #sel-key*. NEEDS in-browser check.
- NOT wired: fretboard, keyboard, director (multiple roots, ambiguous). Mode
  (maj/min) not unified, only the tonic.
- [2026-06-24] *Circle of fifths NOW wired* (canvas, no <select>): not an IIFE, so
  a later script hooks its globals — wrapped ~draw()~ to publish
  ~FIFTHS_TO_CHROMA[selectedRoot]~ as the tonic after every redraw, and applies
  external changes via ~selectedRoot~ + ~lockSpelling/syncUI/draw~. So clicking
  G-DO on the circle → tonal assessment opens in G. (Tonic only; in la-based minor
  circle modes the published pc is the major-key root — fine for the major-DO flow.)
- Confirmed: tonal *assessment IS synced* (its #key, pc) — the resting tone follows
  the shared tonic across navigation.
- Injected ~tonality.js~ + a bind line into the 4 partials (~tools/_solfege-keyboard~,
  ~_scales~, ~_chord-sheet~, ~practice/_assessment-embed~).

** [2026-06-24] Tunes steer tonality; track DO + RESTING TONE (redesign)
- ~tonality.js~ rewritten: tracks *three* values in localStorage (tonalDo,
  tonalResting, tonalMode). DO = key-signature major tonic; RESTING TONE = modal
  tonic (do for major, la for minor, etc.); mode = maj/m/dor/mix/...
  ~keyToTonality(K)~ derives all three from an ABC key field
  (resting = K: tonic pc; do = (fifths·7) mod 12 where fifths = majorFifths(tonic)
  + modeOffset). *Verified node*: Bbmaj→DO/REST Bb; Gm→DO Bb/REST G; Ddor→DO C/
  REST D; Dmix→DO G/REST D; Edor→DO D/REST E; Cm→DO Eb/REST C.
- *Tunes are the master*: ~apps/tunes/index.html~ — removed random-on-load
  (refreshSongbook now starts at tune 0); loads tonality.js; ~publishTonality()~
  (called in setTune on every tune change) reads the tune's K: and
  ~Tonality.setFromKey(..,"tunes")~. (Tunes page still iframed, but localStorage is
  shared same-origin so navigation picks it up.)
- Consumers bind to *DO*: ~bindSelect~ → DO (solfège #do-selector, scales
  #root-select, chord-sheet #sel-key, tonal assessment #key); circle of fifths
  hook → getDo/setDo. Resting tone is stored + readable (~Tonality.getResting()~)
  but no tool consumes it yet.
- DATA: K: already encodes mode for every tune (incl. Ddor/Dmix/Edor/Gm/Cm/…), so
  DO+resting derive correctly *with no data edits*. "Updating tunes data" only
  needed for any tune whose K: mode is wrong/missing (can't auto-detect — flag
  individually). Published tonality uses the *written* K: (ignores vis-transpose).

** [2026-06-24] Circle shows resting tone (LA for minor); assessment → minor patterns
- Key realization: the circle's ~selectedRoot~ chroma is the MODE TONIC (= resting
  tone), not DO (steps[0]=0; DO/parent-major = root + fifthsOffset). Earlier hook
  was wrong (published resting as DO) — fixed.
- *Circle of fifths* now consumes ~{resting, mode}~: sets ~selectedRoot~ =
  FIFTHS_TO_CHROMA.indexOf(resting) and ~selectedMode~ via C_MODE map (maj→0,
  m→5 natural minor, dor→1, mix→4, phr→2, lyd→3, loc→7) → displays the resting
  tone with LA solfège for minor. Publishes ~setAll(do,resting,mode)~ on draw
  (do = (resting + fifthsOffset·7) mod 12). Bidirectional, guarded against echo.
- *Tonal assessment* consumer: minor-3rd mode (m/min/aeo/dor/phr/loc — NOT
  mix/lyd) → switch to the minor collection (preserving stepwise↔I-V type via
  COUNTERPART map), and set #key = RESTING tone pc (collections are written so the
  key offset = resting pc for both major & minor). Replaced its DO bindSelect; now
  consume-only.
- solfège #do-selector / scales / chord-sheet still bind DO (the key signature) —
  correct (DO=Bb for a G-minor tune). Resting tone now actively consumed by the
  circle + assessment.

** [2026-06-25] establish() uses per-mode characteristic chords (not always I-IV-V)
- Fixed: establish was hardcoded I–IV–V. Now uses the circle's
  CHARACTERISTIC_CHORD_DEFS per mode via an EST degree-sequence table:
  maj/m: I-IV-V-I ; aeo(nat min): i-iv-bVII-i ; dor: i-bVII-IV-i ;
  phr: i-bVII-bII-i (Phrygian cadence) ; lyd: I-II-V-I ; mix: I-IV-bVII-I ;
  loc: i°-bIII-bVII-i°. Roots/qualities from MODE_DEF (circle MODES). cache-bust
  tonality.js?v=3 across all 8 partials.

- Chord sheet's passive KEY steering (applyFromShared reads Tonality.getDo/Resting/Mode

** [2026-06-26] Tune library: alphabetical default, key-agnostic handling, romans, sort
- Default order is now ALPHABETICAL (records.sort by title in build; affects picker +
  the deluxe's all()[0] initial pick → "Admiral Anderson").
- KEY-AGNOSTIC by default. New flag directive %%key-fixed marks a tune whose K: key is
  canonical (published/common practice). hasDirective() detects flag directives.
  • Key FACET only tallies keyFixed tunes (empty facet is hidden by the picker).
  • Key shown in the results row ONLY for keyFixed tunes.
  • Key FILTER matches keyFixed tunes only.
  • Deluxe Key selector option 0 = the written key BY NAME (e.g. "G"); appends
    " (original)" only when keyFixed. (tonality facet still applies to all — it's mode,
    not absolute key.)
- Roman numerals per tune: DEGREE[] table (major-relative w/ ♭/♯ chromatics, key-
  independent) + chordRootPc/chordQuality/romanOf; build() collects distinct chords →
  rec.chordCount and rec.romans (deduped, ordered by scale degree). Minor casing comes
  from the chord quality (e.g. Gm tune → "i V"). Shown under each title in the picker.
  Verified: Lightly Row I V, Yankee Doodle I IV V, Amazing Grace I II IV ♯iv° V.
- Picker SORT select: A–Z (default) / fewest chords / most chords (by rec.chordCount).
  cache-bust tune-library.js?v=3.

** [2026-06-26] Roman numerals: secondary/applied chords (vii°/V, V/V, V/IV…)
- Existing project roman logic: PitchSpelling.romanNumeral(rootPc,quality,keyCtx) — basic
  per-chord diatonic/chromatic only, NO secondary functions, and NOT loaded on the Chord
  Sheet tool. So tune-library keeps its own self-contained roman logic.
- Switched roman labeling from raw degree to INTRINSIC applied-chord analysis (by chord
  SPELLING, not resolution — the textbook treatment the user wants). appliedRoman():
  • diminished a half-step below a diatonic degree → vii°/<that degree> (♯iv°7 = vii°/V),
  • chromatic major / any dom7 a fifth above a diatonic degree → V/<that degree>
    (II = V/V, I7 = V/IV, VI = V/ii, III = V/vi …).
  Target named via DIATONIC_LABEL{0:I,2:ii,4:iii,5:IV,7:V,9:vi,11:vii°}; target=tonic is
  excluded (the real V / vii°). isDom7 + isChromaticRoman gate the dominant case.
  Verified: Amazing Grace I V/IV IV vii°/V V/V V; 19 tunes have applied chords.
  NOTE: triad-level (V/IV not V7/IV); key-/resolution-independent by design. cache-bust
  tune-library.js?v=4.

** [2026-06-26] PROTOTYPE: in-browser arranger engine (phase 1 of P4)
- Decision: phased — JS text/JSON style engine now, optional SFF/MMA importer later.
- NEW apps/shared/arranger.js: meter-generic style engine. Style = data-driven event list
  [{b:beatIndex, t:"bass"|"chord", d:beats|"bar"}]; events with b>=beatsPerBar are skipped
  so one pattern fits 2/4,3/4,4/4,6/8… Reads TuneChart.parse output (bars+chord onsets,
  beatsPerBar, meterD, unitsPerBeat, playOrder for repeats), schedules via MusicAudio
  (audio.js) noteAt — bass = low root (oct2), chord = close triad(+7th) in oct4. Drives a
  chart-highlight callback off ctx.currentTime via rAF. 3 starter styles: Block / Waltz /
  Pop-ballad. Self-contained chord parser (root+quality+7th). Node-verified scheduling.
- Wired into the Chord Sheet tool: "Accompaniment" select (Soundfont melody = existing
  abcjs path [default, unchanged]; Block/Waltz/Pop = arranger via MusicAudio). togglePlay
  branches on the select; onBar→chart.setLive; stop() stops the arranger. Also removed an
  accidental duplicate listener block. cache-bust arranger.js?v=1; loads audio.js on deluxe.
- NEEDS BROWSER CHECK (headless can't run real-time Web Audio): audible styles + the chart
  bar-highlight advancing during arranger playback. Engine confirmed to SCHEDULE (32 notes)
  + flip play state without errors.
- SOUNDFONT RENDER (done): arranger now plays through the SOUNDFONT, not the osc synth.
  Added Arranger.toAbc(parsed,{style,tempo,transpose,program}) → a one-voice comping ABC
  (bass note + close triad as [stacks], L:1/8, durations in eighths = 8/meterD per beat;
  block = sustained chord segments, waltz/pop = beat-comping via BEAT_RULES; bars 1:1 with
  the chart so abcjs-mm lines up → existing cursor drives chart.setLive). Deluxe: playAbc()
  returns toAbc when a style≠off is selected; plays via the SAME loadForPlay→setTune(
  {soundFontUrl})→play path as melody mode (so transport/cursor reused). KEY FIX: loadForPlay
  sets voicesOff:false in arranger mode (the accompaniment IS the voice — don't mute it).
  Removed the MusicAudio branch + audio.js from the deluxe (toAbc needs neither; Arranger.play
  kept for non-abcjs hosts). Verified the accompaniment ABC parses + renders (48 notes waltz).
- NOT DONE (future): drums, swing feel, intros/fills/endings, per-section variation; arranger
  ABC plays bars ONCE (repeats not expanded yet) + accompaniment-only (no melody voice);
  SFF/MMA import. Needs a real-browser LISTEN (headless can't run Web Audio).

** [2026-06-27] SATB chorale voicing (4-part, common-practice voice-leading)
- NEW apps/shared/satb.js: SATB.voice(symbols, keyPc) → [{s,a,t,b} midi]. Rules (Open Music
  Theory / Kostka&Payne / Aldwell&Schachter): voice RANGES (S60-81,A55-74,T48-67,B40-62);
  SPACING adjacent uppers ≤8ve, T-B wider; no CROSSING (S≥A≥T≥B); DOUBLING root in root
  position, dim→double 3rd, never double the leading tone; no ‖P5/P8/unison (both voices
  moving); resolve LT up (strict in soprano) + chord 7th down by step; minimize motion /
  keep common tones; soft penalties for overlap, direct 5/8 in outer voices, melodic +aug2.
  Algorithm: enumerate valid candidates per chord (ranges/spacing/doubling/no-LT-double) →
  greedy pick by seedCost (1st chord) then transCost (motion + the rules above). Node-verified
  on I IV V7 I / ii V7 I / I vi IV V I / Am: i iv V7 i → ZERO range/crossing/parallel issues;
  LT→tonic + 7th↓ confirmed.
- arranger.js (v2): style "satb" + toAbcSATB() — segments(parsed) splits the chart into chord
  segments (sym+durE+barEnd), SATB.voice() voices them, emits 4 ABC voices (V:1-4, S/A/T
  treble + B bass) → abcjs renders 4 SEPARATE STAVES and plays all 4 via soundfont. transpose
  applied to output midis (voice-led in the original key). Spelling: K:C + flat accidentals
  (readable; key-sig spelling is a later polish).
- Deluxe loads satb.js?v=1 + arranger.js?v=2; "SATB chorale" appears in the Accompaniment
  select (added at runtime from Arranger.styles()). Verified: visible 4-stave render, no errors.
- LATER: per-part instrument transposition (Bb/Eb), inversions/figured
  bass from slash chords, staff playback cursor, repeats. Needs a real-browser LISTEN.

** [2026-06-27] 4-channel mixer (Melody / Chords / Bass / Drums faders)
- abcjs facts (tested): %%MIDI control 7 (CC7 channel volume) is NOT handled by our build
  ("midi cmd not handled: control"); %%MIDI vol N DOES set per-voice velocity; but a note's
  dynamic DECORATION overrides %%MIDI vol. Per-voice inline [L:] LEAKS globally (unusable).
- So volume is applied differently per channel:
  • chords/bass/drums: scale the per-note value. arranger opts.vol{chords,bass,drums,melody}
    (0-100). dynScaled() = nearestDyn(DYNV[dynFor()]*frac) → the metric accent curve attenuated
    by the fader. drums scale DVEL by frac. vol 0 on chords/bass → that voice emits all rests
    (activeFn gate); drums 0 → no drum header.
  • melody: NEW optional voice. arranger opts.melody{body,program,vol}; melodyVoiceLines emits
    V:Mel + %%MIDI program + %%MIDI vol (melody has no decorations so vol works).
- MELODY EXTRACTION (deluxe extractMelody): strip chords/decorations/grace/$/tuplets/slurs/
  repeats/endings, scale L:1/4→1/8, validate via standalone ABCJS.parseOnly (return null on
  fail → graceful no-melody fallback). Transpose first via AbcTranspose (srcAbc) so the melody
  matches the transposed band. Coverage: 92/95 chord-tunes layer cleanly, 3 fall back. Melody
  is PLAYBACK-ONLY (forPlay) — visible Piano/SATB notation stays the clean accompaniment.
- Off-mode ("Soundfont melody"): melody vol→%%MIDI vol, chords→%%MIDI chordvol (×1.27),
  bass→%%MIDI bassvol (×1.27); melody 0 → voicesOff. Style-mode drums slider gates drums.
- UI: replaced the Melody+Drums CHECKBOXES with a .mixer panel of 4 range faders
  (#csd-vol-melody/chords/bass/drums; defaults 90/80/90/80). change → stop (next play applies).
  Verified node: drums0→0 hits, melody0→melody notes drop, chords0+bass0→only melody plays.
  cache-bust arranger.js?v=11. NEEDS A REAL-BROWSER LISTEN for balance.

** [2026-06-27] Dynamics — fix "same volume / loud / amusical"
- All melodic notes were a flat velocity 85 (no metric accent); only drums varied. abcjs maps
  dynamic decorations to velocity (ppp30 pp35 p50 mp65 mf90 f95 ff110 fff125) and a dynamic
  persists until the next. %%MIDI beat misfired (made everything one value). Fix:
  • arranger dynFor(role,sl,M): downbeat→f, on-beat→mp, offbeat→p; bass down→f else mp;
    arp beat→mp else p. Emitted as !x! decorations, GATED on opts.dynamics.
  • %%MIDI nobeataccents in the playback header so OUR dynamics are the only velocity source
    (else abcjs double-scales → offbeats ~35, too soft). Result: distinct 50/65/95.
  • DVEL eased (kick 105→98, snare 100→90, hats 55→46…) so drums sit under the softer harmony.
- KEEP DYNAMICS OUT OF THE PRINTED NOTATION: dynamic decorations would render as mf/p marks on
  every note. So toAbc takes opts.dynamics; deluxe renders the CLEAN ABC (dynamics:false) into
  the visible #csd-notation and a SEPARATE dynamics ABC (playAbc(true) → #csd-hidden-notation)
  for synth.setTune. renderNotation()=display only; loadForPlay()=its own played visualObj.
  Verified: displayNotes 48 / dynamicMarks 0; playback velocities {50,65,95}. cache-bust
  arranger.js?v=10. NEEDS A REAL-BROWSER LISTEN to fine-tune levels.

** [2026-06-27] Drums (channel-10 %%MIDI drum; no staff)
- User: the styles "fall flat without drums." abcjs plays %%MIDI drum once you also emit
  %%MIDI drumon (drumon/drumoff are param0 MIDI cmds; drum is param1String+variable ints:
  pattern d/z chars + one GM-note per d + one velocity per d). Node-verified it produces
  channel-10 note-ons and adds NO staff (so the Piano/SATB notation is untouched).
- arranger.js: DRUM token→GM note map (k36 s38 h42 o46 r51 x37 c49 t54) + DVEL accents
  (kick/snare loud, hats soft). drumGroove(styleId,M): per-style `drum` override, else a
  waltz groove "kzhzhz" when beats===3, else backbeat "khsh"; tiled one token per eighth
  across M.barE (meter-generic). drumHeader() emits ["%%MIDI drumon","%%MIDI drum …"];
  toAbcPiano/toAbcSATB splice it into the header when opts.drums. Do NOT emit "%%MIDI
  channel 10" (it would hijack the melodic voices — the drum directive auto-uses ch10).
- Deluxe: "Drums" checkbox (default ON) → playAbc passes drums; toggle stops playback so the
  next play picks it up (header-only, no notation change). cache-bust arranger.js?v=8.
- Verified node: piano+folk 16 ch10 hits, satb layers drums (4 staves, no drum staff), 3/4
  waltz drum = kick+hat+hat, drums:false = none. NEEDS A REAL-BROWSER LISTEN (headless can't
  run Web Audio).
- [2026-06-27] GENRE DRUM KITS added via per-style `drum` cells. Can't layer (one %%MIDI drum
  line) so feels INTERLEAVE (ride/hat fill gaps): offbeat→reggae one-drop "zhzhkhzh" (kick on
  3); bossa→"xrrxrrxr" (ride + side-stick 3+3+2 tresillo); tango→"khhkhhkh" (habanera kick
  tresillo + hats); charleston→"rzrrrzrr" + jazzwaltz→"rzrrrz" (swing ride); driving8→"khshkhsh".
  Default backbeat "khsh"; waltz "kzhzhz" when beats===3 (viennese). Node-verified distinct +
  still 2 staves (no drum staff). FULL LAYERING needs a perc voice (clef=perc + chords) but
  that ALWAYS renders a staff (omitting from %%score didn't hide it) — would need CSS-hiding or
  a 2nd synth; deferred. cache-bust arranger.js?v=9. Still needs a real-browser LISTEN.

** [2026-06-27] gchord-pattern styles (12 new, from Eskin/abc2midi vocabulary)
- Source: a michaeleskin.com/abctools share link (def= is URL-safe-base64 + zlib of ABC)
  with 55 accompaniment styles defined via %%MIDI gchord + gchordstress + gchordduration +
  chordprog/bassprog. Our abcjs has gchord/gchordduration but NOT gchordstress (velocity).
- Chose to TRANSLATE the gchord vocabulary into our explicit-note engine (keeps written
  Piano/SATB notation + key-aware spelling + see=hear), NOT native %%MIDI gchord (which would
  play but render no written parts).
- arranger.js: TOK map (z rest / f bass / c chord / b bass+chord / g h i j k arpeggio tones
  ascending / G H I J octave-down). TWO style flavours:
    • `cell` = a short token cell tiled ONE TOKEN PER EIGHTH across the bar → METER-GENERIC
      (folk "fc", oompah "bc", driving8 "cc", offbeat "zc", bassroot "fz", arpcell "gi").
    • `gchord` = a full-bar pattern for a specific `meter` (charleston, bossa, tango, arpflow
      4/4; viennese, jazzwaltz 3/4) — label carries the meter; mismatch → block.
  barHits → patternFor() picks cell (any meter) or gchord (matching meter), else block;
  barHitsCell/barHitsPattern build slots; partial/pickup bars fall back to blockSlots().
  emitVoice clamps each note to the next own-hit (per-slot durations → staccato/legato + gaps).
  Piano RH plays arp tones (voicing[idx]+oct); SATB treats arp as a chord onset. cache-bust v7.
- BUG FIXED (user: "all sound/render as block chords"): the first cut made every pattern a
  full-bar 4/4 gchord, but the library is mostly 2/4 (45 tunes), 6/8 (16), 2/2 (8) — so almost
  everything fell back to block. Fix = meter-generic per-eighth `cell` styles. Verified the 6
  cells produce distinct output on 2/4, 3/4, 4/4 AND 6/8 (the default tune), note-counts differ
  per style in-browser (block 24 / folk 48 / oompah 72 …), no errors.
- NOT YET ported: the syncopated/fractional styles (31-55 use 0.25/0.5/0.75-eighth stress &
  duration → need a 16th grid); the 6/8 Afro-Cuban; per-style GM instruments (chordprog/bassprog
  → would map to %%MIDI program per voice); velocity (gchordstress; our abcjs lacks it).

** [2026-06-27] Split accompaniment STYLE from NOTATION (two independent axes)
- "SATB" was wrongly an accompaniment STYLE alongside block/waltz/pop. Now:
  • STYLE (rhythm): block / waltz / pop  (Arranger.styles()).
  • NOTATION (layout): "piano" (grand staff: RH triad + LH bass, %%score {1 2}) or
    "satb" (4 voice-led staves). (Arranger.notations()). toAbc dispatches on opts.notation.
- Refactor: one shared rhythm engine — metrics(), barHits(bar,style,M) → position-tiling
  {pos,dur,bass,chord,sym} slots; emitVoice(hits,activeFn,tokFn) fills a voice w/ notes+rests.
  toAbcPiano (RH=chord slots, LH=bass slots) and toAbcSATB (S/A/T=chord slots, B=bass slots,
  voice-led via satb.js, voicingAt(bar,pos)) both consume it → style × notation fully
  independent (e.g. SATB+waltz = voices articulate the oom-pah-pah; Piano+block = sustained).
  Keeps key-aware spelling + tenor-clef option.
- Deluxe: separate "Accompaniment" (off/block/waltz/pop) + "Notation" (Piano/SATB) selects.
  GOTCHA fixed: the notation SELECT first collided with the notation STAFF DIV (both
  id="csd-notation"); renamed the select to #csd-notesel. cache-bust arranger.js?v=5.

** [2026-06-27] SATB tenor clef: treble-8 (fixes "octave too low") or bass, user choice
- The tenor read an octave low because it was on a plain treble clef at sounding pitch.
  abcjs's clef=treble-8 is COSMETIC (verticalPos 0, mid:0 — same as treble + an "8" glyph;
  does NOT shift display or MIDI). So a real vocal-tenor clef = write the tenor +12 (so it
  sits in the treble staff) with clef=treble-8, and a PER-VOICE %%MIDI transpose -12 to keep
  the sound at pitch. Verified via getMidiFile bytes: %%MIDI transpose is per-voice and does
  NOT leak to later voices (3-voice test), and both clef modes produce IDENTICAL sounding
  pitches.
- toAbcSATB opts.tenorClef: "treble8" (default; +12 written, clef=treble-8, %%MIDI transpose
  -12) or "bass" (clef=bass, at pitch). voiceBody(role, octShift) adds the display octave.
  Deluxe: new "Tenor" select (Treble 8 / Bass clef) in the transport → playAbc passes
  tenorClef; change re-renders. cache-bust arranger.js?v=4.

** [2026-06-27] Arranger notation: key-aware pitch spelling + key signatures
- The accompaniment ABC was flat-only + K:C. Now uses the project's spelling: loads
  pitch-spelling.js on the deluxe; arranger keyContext(parsed,transpose) derives the key
  (fifths from keyField + mode) → centerLof, per-letter key signature, K: = relative-major
  name (same sig; abcjs shows the sig, not a name).
- CHORD-AWARE spelling (key-centered alone mis-spelled chromatic chord tones — A7 in Dm
  gave Db). chordSpellingMap(sym) spells each tone by FUNCTION off the chord-root letter
  (root/3rd/5th/7th + slash bass) → A7 = A C# E G. Falls back to PitchSpelling key-centered
  for non-chord pcs and for transposed playback (map is built from the untransposed symbol).
- spellNote() emits minimal accidentals: ^/_/= only when the note differs from the key sig
  or the in-measure state (ms, reset each barline); octave from the spelled LETTER (handles
  Cb/B#). Used by BOTH the block/waltz/pop comping AND SATB; K: set on both. Node-verified:
  G→F#, F→Bb, Dm A7→C#, E-major sharps; all reparse + key sig renders. Fallback to flats+K:C
  if PitchSpelling absent. cache-bust: deluxe loads pitch-spelling.js + arranger.js?v=3.

** [2026-06-27] Chord Sheet: VISIBLE accompaniment notation (+ ready for flex-ensemble parts)
- The accompaniment ABC was rendered to an off-screen div; now there's a VISIBLE cream
  staff panel (#csd-notation, .csd-notation) in .stand below the transport. renderNotation():
  style≠off → render playAbc() into #csd-notation (display block) and return its visualObj;
  style=off → keep it hidden and render melody into the off-screen #csd-hidden-notation (chord
  sheet stays clean by default). loadForPlay uses renderNotation()'s visualObj (what you see =
  what you hear). Called live on tune/style/key change. Verified: waltz → visible staff, 48
  notes, no errors.
- READY FOR MULTI-PART (flex ensemble): abcjs renders multi-voice ABC (V:1/V:2/…) as stacked
  staves automatically, so when Arranger.toAbc emits N voices they'll appear on separate lines
  here with no view changes. NEXT: extend toAbc with a `parts` option that distributes chord
  tones across N singable lines (+ ranges/voice-leading/per-part transposition); optional staff
  playback cursor. cache-bust: deluxe is inline (re-renders); arranger.js?v=1 unchanged.

** [2026-06-26] RESEARCH: open-source arranger/auto-accompaniment options (P4 backlog)
Goal: build the accompaniment-styles engine on existing work, not from scratch. Finding:
NO browser/JS arranger engine exists to drop in — the reusable value is the STYLE
FORMATS + their free libraries; build a small JS engine that consumes one.
- ENGINES (all desktop, wrong runtime to embed):
  • MMA "Musical MIDI Accompaniment" — Python, GPL, ~2000 styles, PLAIN-TEXT groove
    format (easy to parse/author), outputs SMF. mellowood.ca/mma. BandInMuseScore wraps
    it. Best for a hand-authorable text style format.
  • JJazzLab + YamJJazz — Java, open source, reads Yamaha .sty/.prs (SFF1/SFF2). Desktop.
    The de-facto reference implementation for parsing Yamaha styles. jjazzlab.org.
  • Ensembles — Vala/GTK + FluidSynth, Linux, 100+ styles.
  • One Man Band / vArranger / Band-in-a-Box / Chordpulse / Strum Machine — closed.
- FORMATS / DATA (the actual reuse target):
  • Yamaha SFF (.sty) = SMF0 MIDI patterns (Intro/Main A-D/Fill/Ending markers) + a CASM
    section (per-part chord-transposition/voicing rules). Binary but documented (jososoft
    style2_*, wierzba StyleFileDescription_v21.pdf). HUGE free library (psrtutorial). JS
    binary parsing is feasible — the in-browser AUS→STY converter (ArrayBuffer/Uint8Array)
    proves it. Richest ecosystem; most parsing work (CASM).
  • MMA grooves = text, GPL data. Easiest to parse + lets the teacher author styles.
- BUILDING BLOCKS for a homegrown JS engine: Tone.js (scheduling/transport), Tonal.js
  (chord/voicing math). We already have: TuneChart (bar/beat/chord data) + soundfont
  player → an engine just maps (chord × style pattern) → scheduled notes.
- LICENSE flags: MMA GPL (reuse groove DATA w/ attribution; keep our engine code ours);
  JJazzLab/YamJJazz open but Java (study, don't port); .sty FORMAT is open knowledge but
  individual style FILES vary in copyright — curate sources.
- RECOMMENDED PATH: small JS "style engine" with a simple text/JSON pattern format
  (MMA-inspired), feeding the existing soundfont player; later add an optional SFF
  importer to pull in the Yamaha library. NEEDS USER DECISION before building.

** [2026-06-26] Chart: toggle roman numerals instead of letter chords
- TuneChart render gains a `roman` option + setRoman(on): when on, each chord cell shows
  TuneLibrary.roman(sym, parsed.keyPc) (the applied-chord roman) instead of fmtChord.
  Romans are transposition-invariant, so the Key/transpose state is ignored in roman
  mode. Reused tune-library's roman (exported as TuneLibrary.roman = appliedRoman) rather
  than duplicating — TuneLibrary is loaded on both chart pages; graceful fallback to the
  letter symbol if absent. CSS .tc-roman-mode shrinks .tc-rt + uses JetBrains Mono.
- Toggle added on BOTH: Chord Sheet transport (#csd-roman → chart.setRoman) and the
  assessment Chord-chart panel (#chartRoman → renderChart passes roman:chartRomanOn()).
  Verified: Amazing Grace cells G G7 C C♯dim7 → I V/IV IV vii°/V … . cache-bust
  tune-chart.js?v=5, tune-library.js?v=5, app.js?v=21.

** [2026-06-26] Reading Benchmarks: separate file + dedicated page (no chart/playback)
- Reading benchmarks aren't tunes → moved out of tunes-object.js into
  apps/tunes/reading-benchmarks-object.js (var readingBenchmarks {set: bigAbcString};
  added `window.readingBenchmarks=` since a top-level const isn't a window prop). Added
  stable %%id (rb-1a-N) to each line. tunesObject now back to 3 collections.
- app.js is now CONFIG-AWARE + null-safe so it can be reused without playback/chart:
    window.TUNE_APP_CONFIG = { source, playback, storeKey }   (set before app.js loads)
  TUNES = window[source]||tunesObject; ALLOW_PLAYBACK=playback!==false (setupSynth
  bails when off OR when #tune-audio absent); saved-state key = storeKey. Null-guarded
  every #chords / #chordVol / #metroVol read + listener and chordsOn()/chordVol() so an
  embed can omit those controls. renderChart already no-ops when #tuneChart absent.
- New page practice/reading-benchmarks.qmd + _reading-benchmarks-embed.qmd: assessment
  tool only — NO chord chart, NO preview playback (config playback:false; omits Play/
  tune-audio/chords/voices/cycle/transpose/chart). Keeps mic/record/grade + 2-bar
  count-off + metronome + tolerances. Establish-tonality KEPT (key reference is standard
  before sight-reading; easy to drop if unwanted). Nav: under Practice, after Tune.
  Verified both pages + the deluxe tool render clean, no JS errors.

** [2026-06-26] Anacrusis = measure 0, first FULL bar = measure 1 (+ layout config)
- tune-chart.js parse() now flags a leading partial bar as anacrusis (parsed.anacrusis,
  anacrusisUnits) and numbers bars measureNo (pickup=0, first full bar=1). Verified
  32/108 tunes have a real pickup (e.g. Rattling Bog starts "d/e/|").
- Chart render: the pickup is no longer a full grid cell — it's a "⤴ PICKUP" flag at the
  top-left (in extra top padding via .tc-has-pickup), carrying the pickup's chord if any.
  The flag IS barEls[0] so the live bar sits on it while the anacrusis plays; the grid
  proper starts at measure 1. barEls is now indexed BY BAR INDEX (sparse-safe) so
  playOrder/setPlayMeasure still map correctly.
- Layout config (hard-codable per tune in the ABC, opts override at render):
  %%score-bars N  → N measures/line;  %%score-breaks a,b,c → explicit per-line counts.
  render buildRows() honors lineBreaks else barsPerRow (default 4).
- Recording count-off reworked: now 2 BARS (was COUNT_IN=4 quarters). barQ=meter*4/d,
  countInQ=2*barQ, accentEvery=round(barQ) (fixes bar-accent for 6/8 etc.). With a
  pickup, target onset 0 sounds at (2 bars − anaQ) so measure 1's downbeat lands on the
  bar-3 click; caption tells the user to "come in on the pickup in bar 2". anaQ from the
  shared TuneChart parse (single source of truth). cache-bust tune-chart.js?v=3, app.js?v=18.
- FUTURE (designed-for, not built): mid-tune time/key signature changes. parse() is
  meter/L-driven and per-bar; would extend bars[] with per-bar meter + recompute
  unitsPerBar/beat locally, and the count-off/grid already key off per-bar lengths.

** [2026-06-26] Chart: highlight on both pages, repeat fix, cycle on chord-sheet
- PARSER BUG FIX (this also fixed "repeats don't work"): `|:` mid-stream wasn't tagging
  the bar — the preceding `:|` flush already created the next `cur` with left="plain"
  before `|:` set pendingLeft. Now `|:` also sets `cur.left="repeat-open"`. So section-B
  `|:` repeat barlines RENDER (they didn't before) AND playOrder is correct.
- computePlayOrder(bars): expands |: … :| (+ implicit start-of-tune open, ::, 1st-ending
  skip) into the WRITTEN-bar sequence playback visits. Node-checked: Sportsman/Rattling
  Bog/Childgrove all expand to proper AABB. Exposed as parsed.playOrder + a render method
  setPlayMeasure(m) that maps the m-th played measure → written bar → setLive.
- Highlight now driven by setPlayMeasure on BOTH pages: deluxe cursor.onEvent and the
  assessment cursorControl.onEvent count measureStarts (chartMeasure) and call
  setPlayMeasure, so the live bar follows repeats correctly instead of running off the end.
- Cycle added to the Chord Sheet tool (#csd-cycle → synth.isLooping, re-applied after
  setTune since it resets). Assessment already had Cycle.
- cache-bust tune-chart.js?v=2, app.js?v=17.
- NOT browser-verifiable here: headless Chrome won't run real-time Web Audio (virtual
  time doesn't drive the audio clock), so the live-highlight-through-repeats + cycle need
  a real-browser check. Watch the pickup/anacrusis case: if the highlight is off by one
  measure on tunes with a pickup, abcjs's measureStart count differs from playOrder and
  we'd switch to mapping via ev.startChar instead of counting.

** [2026-06-26] Shared chord-chart module + beat lines + assessment-page chart
- NEW shared/tune-chart.js (TuneChart.parse/render) — ONE backend file for the chart,
  used by BOTH the Chord Sheet tool and the Tune Assessment page (per user: keep chart
  code shared). Self-injects scoped `.tune-chart` CSS + the Oswald/JetBrains fonts.
- parse() now tracks NOTE DURATIONS (L-unit math: handles num/den durations, "/"//,
  broken rhythm > <, tuplets (p, [chord] groups, grace {}, rests) so every chord gets
  an onset IN BEATS. Generic for any meter (M: n/d, C, C|, none) and L (1/4,1/8) —
  computes unitsPerBar/unitsPerBeat/beatsPerBar. Node-validated: 1060 interior bars sum
  exactly to unitsPerBar; the ~134 "off" ones are all anacrusis / repeat-split bars
  (correct). Meters in the data incl. 5/8, 7/8, 6/8 → generic was essential.
- Chords are now ABSOLUTELY positioned at left = onset/unitsPerBar (beat-accurate,
  multi-chord bars line up). Toggleable sub-bar "beat lines" (.tc-subline) every N
  beats, drawn only within a bar's filled length. Off/1/2 via a select on each page.
- Chord Sheet tool: ripped out its inline parseBars/fmtChord/renderChart; now calls
  TuneChart.render + setLive/setSubBars/setTranspose. Added a "Beat lines" select.
- Tune Assessment: added a "Chord chart" panel (TuneChart, transpose=keyTrans concert,
  preferFlats=!keyAlt). Made Setup/Notation/Chord-chart each a collapsible <details>
  (.ta-panel) so you can view both or collapse either. renderTune() calls renderChart().
  cache-bust app.js?v=16, tune-chart.js?v=1.

** [2026-06-25] NEW TOOL: Chord Sheet — Stage Edition (iRealPro-style + playback)
- tools/chord-sheet-deluxe.qmd (wrapper) + tools/_chord-sheet-deluxe.qmd (the tool).
  The old _chord-sheet-deluxe was just a restyled copy of the chord-DIAGRAM tool; fully
  replaced with the real thing. Nav entry already in _quarto.yml.
- What it does: set-list rail (collection + every tune) → iRealPro-ish measure-grid
  chord chart (4 bars/row, real barlines/repeats/endings, beat-slash for empty bars) →
  transport that plays the whole tune via the SAME abcjs SynthController + MusyngKite
  soundfont as the tune assessment (piano/nylon/bass GM band; bass+chords always,
  melody optional). Tempo slider, Key transpose (uses shared/abc-transpose.js), and a
  teal "live" bar that travels measure-by-measure via cursor.onEvent measureStart.
- Self-contained IIFE; parses tunesObject directly (own parseBars). No grading, no mic.
- QUARTO GOTCHA (cost a lot of debugging — remember this): inside `::: {.column-screen}`
  Quarto stamps `page-columns page-full` onto your CONTAINER divs AND, via
  `.page-columns.page-full > *`, sets `grid-column:<named page lines>` on their direct
  CHILDREN. If your container is your own CSS grid, those non-existent named lines spawn
  phantom tracks (saw `grid-template-columns: 248px 178px 0px 610px`) and the layout
  collapses/offsets. FIX = (a) force the decorated containers back to display:block, and
  (b) on your real grid, `#csd .stage>*{grid-column:auto!important}` to wipe Quarto's
  child placement. Higher-specificity (#id .cls.page-columns) + !important to win the
  cascade. The original chord-sheet sidesteps this by keeping ALL layout inside one
  undecorated #...-root with no top-level grid.
- Headless-screenshot tip: Chrome on Retina defaults to DPR=2, so --window-size=1280
  becomes 640 CSS px and trips mobile breakpoints; pass --force-device-scale-factor=1.

** [2026-06-25] Tune assessment = hub: nav, shared steer, concert/transposed everywhere
- NAV (_quarto.yml): unlisted practice/tunes.qmd (original tunes app); moved
  practice/assessment-tune.qmd to the TOP of Practice.
- tonality.js (v4): added a SHARED instrument + view store — getInstrument/setInstrument
  ({name,semis} via INSTRUMENTS map), getView/setView ("concert"|"transposed"),
  storage-synced. Cross-page propagation is via localStorage (next page reads on load)
  + storage events; in-page src "local"/"tune" are ignored by their own onChange.
- Tune page (app.js v13): steerTonality() publishes the SOUNDING (concert) key on
  tune load + concert/transposed key change (so circle/patterns follow). Remembers the
  transposing instrument (restores instSel from Tonality.getInstrument; instSel change
  → setInstrument). Reacts to external key/instrument changes (onChange, src!="tune").
- Patterns assessment: "Key"→"Concert Key" + Transposed Key menu + View(Concert/
  Transposed). renderPattern adds the instrument offset to visualTranspose ONLY in
  transposed view (targets/grading/sound stay concert). #key change now steers the
  shared tonality. (assessment app.js v8; glue in _assessment-embed.qmd)
- Circle of fifths: injected #cof-keybar (Concert Key / Transposed Key / View). Shared
  store stays CONCERT; transposed view highlights the read key (concert+instSemis) and
  publish() subtracts instSemis to store concert. (inline glue in _circle-of-fifths.qmd)
- NEEDS BROWSER CHECK: cross-page steering (set key/instrument on tune → circle &
  patterns follow on navigation) and the transposed-view displays.

** [2026-06-25] Tune: enharmonic STAFF (abc-transpose wired) + fixes
- FIX notation-blank bug: syncKeyUI() ran at init before the const instAdjust/INST
  existed → TDZ throw halted the whole script → nothing rendered. Moved syncKeyUI()
  to the end (after the const helpers, before loadCollection).
- shared/abc-transpose.js: line-of-fifths transposer (notes + chord symbols + K:),
  caller picks the spelling. Node-verified: every tune to every sensible
  (<=7-accidental) key with 100% correct pitches.
- WIRED: previewAbc transposes currentAbc to the chosen WRITTEN key
  (keyNameAscii(writtenRootPc + keyTrans + instAdjust), spelling per keyAlt) when
  shift!=0 or keyAlt; renders at visualTranspose:0 (transpose baked into the ABC). So
  the STAFF key sig + accidentals now follow Concert/Transposed key AND ⇄ Enharmonic
  (F#<->Gb, Cb, ...). midiTranspose=-instAdjust keeps SOUND at concert; grading still
  on original notes + keyTrans. Enharmonic button re-renders.
- Cycle instructions: #cycleHint under the notation updates on Cycle toggle / region
  clicks. cache-bust app.js?v=12, abc-transpose.js?v=1.

** [2026-06-25] Tune: DAW region cycle (loop a span)
- Cycle checkbox now = region loop. With Cycle on, click two noteheads → loop In/Out
  (loopInMs/loopOutMs from each note's currentTrackMilliseconds; 3rd click restarts
  the region). cursorControl.onEvent: when ev.milliseconds>=loopOut → seek(loopIn/dur).
  Region notes tinted purple (.loop-region). No region set + Cycle on → whole-tune
  loop (applyCycle: isLooping when no region). Region cleared on tune/tempo/key change
  (timing changes ⇒ stale ms). cache-bust tune app.js?v=10.
- STILL TODO: staff enharmonic (flip the STAFF key sig F#↔Gb). abcjs transposeKey is
  fixed-spelling-per-pc, so needs a custom ABC transposer (notes + chord symbols +
  K:, line-of-fifths spelling). Will write + VERIFY IN NODE (transpose→reparse→compare
  MIDI for all tunes) before shipping. Keep grading on original notes + keyTrans.

** [2026-06-25] Tune: Concert/Transposed key, enharmonic toggle, cycle
- "Key" → "Concert Key". Transposed Key dropdown (#transKey, label "Transposed Key
  (<inst>)") shows only for transposing instruments; mirrors the concert selection
  (transposed = concert + instAdjust); both write the same concert pc → bidirectional
  via syncKeyUI(). Like the chord-sheet's paired dropdowns.
- "⇄ Enharmonic" button flips key-name spelling via ENHARM[pc]=[default,alt] incl.
  C♭(B), B♯(C), E♯(F), F♭(E). LABELS ONLY — abcjs transposeKey picks one spelling per
  pc (no enharmonic option), so the STAFF key-signature spelling isn't flipped.
  Caveat noted; would need SVG post-processing to sync the staff.
- "🔁 Cycle" = whole-tune loop (synthControl.isLooping, re-applied after each setTune
  since setTune resets it). NOT yet DAW region-cycle (in/out points) — TODO: set a
  loop region by clicking two noteheads + seek-back-on-tick.
  cache-bust tune app.js?v=9.

** [2026-06-25] Tune: drop info line, SPACE=play/pause always, Key + Instrument
- Removed the #tuneInfo line ("N notes · M/M · written tempo ♩=…") (markup + JS).
- SPACE = play/pause even when a menu/slider/checkbox is focused: keydown guard now
  only skips real text inputs; else preventDefault + blur(focused) + hearTune().
- Added Instrument (display-only) + Key to the top controls. Instrument map = the
  original's INST{adj,clef}; Key = note-name select (or "As written"), transpose vs
  the tune's written DO (computed in loadTune via Tonality.keyToTonality).
  visualTranspose = keyTrans + instAdjust (display); midiTranspose = keyTrans (sound
  = concert; abcjs visualTranspose is display-only for the synth, confirmed by the
  original's formula); GRADING + record chords shifted by keyTrans (concert), NOT
  instAdjust (so a transposing instrument is purely a reading aid). Instrument also
  sets clef=bass (Trombone/Tuba) in previewAbc. cache-bust tune app.js?v=8.
  NEEDS BROWSER CHECK: transpose direction/octave for transposing instruments.

** [2026-06-25] Fix notehead-seek + hide the abcjs widget grey band
- Noteheads weren't clickable: renderAbc had selectTypes:false (disables selectable
  elements → clickListener never fires). Removed it. Now onNoteClick fires + seeks.
- Grey band = abcjs .abcjs-inline-audio widget (26px, #424242). Hid the whole widget
  (#tune-audio{display:none}); play/pause via the button+SPACE, seek via noteheads,
  tempo via the slider — the hidden control still works through the API. NOTE: this
  also hides the loop/restart/warp UI; add a custom loop toggle later if wanted.
  cache-bust tune app.js?v=7.

** [2026-06-25] Tune transport: drop progress bar, notehead seek, Play/Pause + SPACE
- SynthController load opts: displayProgress:false + displayPlay:false (progress bar
  removed per user; widget keeps loop/restart/warp). The #play button is the
  play/pause: hearTune() toggles synthControl.play(), label "▶ Play tune" / "⏸ Pause"
  via previewPlaying + updatePlayLabel (reset on onFinished / stopPlayback).
- SPACE now bound to play/pause (hearTune), not stopAll (guards INPUT/SELECT/BUTTON
  so a focused #play doesn't double-fire).
- Click a notehead → seek: renderAbc clickListener=onNoteClick reads
  abcElem.currentTrackMilliseconds, converts to a fraction of midiBuffer.duration,
  calls synthControl.seek(frac)+setProgress. Builds the buffer first via
  synthControl.go() if not loaded (currentTrackMilliseconds is only set after the
  buffer/timing is built). Doesn't auto-play — next play starts from there.
  cache-bust tune app.js?v=6.

** [2026-06-25] FIX root cause of stuck preview: abcjs isLoaded guard
- The disable(true) attempt didn't fix it. Real cause: abcjs SynthController
  runWhenReady() only calls go() (which REBUILDS the audio buffer from the current
  visualObj/options) when !isLoaded; after the first play isLoaded stays true and
  setTune never resets it → every later play reused the FIRST buffer (stuck on the
  first tune/tempo/instruments). FIX in renderTune: drop the throwaway CreateSynth,
  pass {soundFontUrl} to setTune, and set synthControl.isLoaded=false after setTune
  so the next play rebuilds with the current settings. cache-bust tune app.js?v=5.

** [2026-06-25] Tune preview fixes: stuck-playback + controls now drive preview
- BUG (stuck / replays old tune on tune change): renderTune didn't reset the
  transport. FIX: synthControl.disable(true) at the top of renderTune before
  re-setTune (mirrors the tunes app) → loading a new tune resets cleanly.
- Tempo slider + voice menus only affected RECORD before. FIX: previewAbc() injects
  Q:1/4=<slider> + %%MIDI program (melody GM) / chordprog (accompaniment GM) /
  bassprog into the ABC the abcjs synth plays; voices map to GM via
  GM={piano:0,epiano:4,nylon:24,bass:32,vibraphone:11,marimba:12}. tempo/melVoice/
  accVoice change → renderTune re-cues. So both controls now drive preview AND
  record (preview = soundfont GM, record = MusicAudio synth voices — same SELECTION,
  different engine). cache-bust tune app.js?v=4.

** [2026-06-25] Tune assessment ← tunes-app playback (transport + cursor)
- Preview "Hear tune" now runs through abcjs SynthController (soundfont MusyngKite +
  auto bass/chord accompaniment from chord symbols). The #tune-audio widget gives
  Loop / Restart / Progress+scrub / Warp; a custom cursorControl highlights notes
  on #notation (renders add_classes:true; .highlight overrides the forced-black fill;
  .abcjs-cursor line). Added "🎵 Play melody" toggle (voicesOff) + the existing
  Chords toggle (chordsOff) re-setTune. #play + SPACE toggle synthControl.play/pause;
  record start / stopAll pause the synth. Loaded /apps/shared/abcjs-audio.css.
  NOTE: preview = soundfont (abcjs); the RECORD play-along still = MusicAudio
  (metronome click + chords + bass) for grading sync, so melVoice/accVoice apply to
  the play-along, not the preview. cache-bust tune app.js?v=3.
- STILL TODO from the tunes-app feature list (transposition entangles GRADING — mic
  captures CONCERT pitch): Key selector, Octaves shift, Instrument transposition

** [2026-06-25] Audio overhaul — done + BACKLOG (prioritized)
*** DONE this pass
- MusicAudio voices = the requested 6: piano, epiano (Electric piano), nylon
  (Nylon guitar), bass, vibraphone, marimba. voiceList() returns {id,label}.
- Tune assessment ported off raw oscillators onto MusicAudio (shares MusicAudio.ctx
  so the count-in/record scheduling still lines up): melody + accompaniment chords +
  a BASS note (chord root dropped to the bass register) + click() metronome. Added
  Melody voice / Accompaniment voice menus; bass line always uses "bass". STOP now
  calls MusicAudio.stopAll() (added source tracking) to cut scheduled notes.
- establish-tonality now plays the PRIMARY chords (I–IV–V–I) of the CURRENT tonality,
  built from the circle's per-mode scale steps + chord qualities (minor=harmonic →
  major V; dorian → major IV; etc.). [[tonality.js]] MODE_DEF/TRIAD.
- cache-bust: audio.js?v=3, tonality.js?v=2, tune app.js?v=2.
*** BACKLOG / remind me
- [P1] Limited synth-control menu: reverb amount, brightness/attack, master vol.
  (Voice menus are the first slice.)
- [P2] Soundfont OPTION (toggle synth↔samples) + better free soundfont. See research
  below. Likely surikov/webaudiofont or danigb/smplr loading FluidR3/GeneralUser.
- [P3] Drum app (apps/chord-library/drumset.html): load drum sounds from a soundfont
  instead of local samples; expose those drum sounds as METRONOME options.
- [P4] Accompaniment STYLES (keyboard-arranger): beyond the current boom-chick — a
  library of styles (waltz/swing/bossa/rock/…) applied to ALL accompaniment
  instruments, chord-driven. No off-the-shelf JS arranger → build a small style
  engine (per-style rhythmic/voicing templates fed by the chord symbols). Tonal.js
  can help with voicings.
- [P5] Drumset patterns (depends on P3 + the style engine in P4).
- [RESOURCE WATCH] MusicAudio uses a convolution reverb (1 shared convolver) + per
  note ~2-3 osc + filter + gains; chords(+bass+melody) every beat = many concurrent
  voices. Fine so far, but on long tunes / low-end devices watch CPU. Levers if it
  bites: shorten/disable the reverb IR, cap polyphony, simpler filter.

** [2026-06-25] Better shared synth (audio.js v2) — foundation for tune-assessment audio
- Rewrote MusicAudio: detuned osc stack → per-note low-pass filter w/ its own
  (brighter-on-attack) envelope → ADSR amp → master bus (DynamicsCompressor +
  convolution reverb from a generated IR). 6 curated voices (piano/epiano/strings/
  pluck/flute/vibes) instead of a GM list; added click() (band-passed noise tick)
  for metronomes and setVoice()/voices(). API back-compatible (note/noteAt/chord/
  ctx/midiToFreq) so establish/chord-sheet/circle just sound better. cache-bust
  audio.js?v=2 across all 8 partials that load it.
- NEXT (pending user eval of the new sound + voice choice): port the tune assessment
  off its raw oscillator tone() onto this engine — melody + ADVANCED ACCOMPANIMENT
  (bass + chords from the chord symbols, like the original tunes app) + click()
  metronome, sharing MusicAudio.ctx() so the precise count-in/record scheduling
  still lines up. Add a SHORT curated instrument selector. (User: "do better than
  current soundfonts; definitely improve osc synthesis" — synth-first, samples later.)
