** [2026-06-24] Reserve headroom for the octave bracket (no staff shift)
- The staff appeared to "shift down" when the 8va/8vb bracket/annotation was added.
  FIX: added ~%%topspace 22~ to BOTH the original (v1RenderAbc) and the response ABC
  → constant headroom above the staff. The bracket now lives in that reserved space,
  and the original + response staves stay aligned whether or not 8vb shows, so the
  staff position is stable. (22 is tunable.) cache-bust app.js?v=6.

** [2026-06-24] Notation: stable two-pane + fill pane width
- The "size change after response" was the :has() collapse (full-width single →
  two half-panes on response), which also contradicted the spec "original only ever
  in the left half." REMOVED the collapse: score-row is always 2-col, so the
  original stays left-half and never resizes; response fills the right (empty until
  recorded). Removed the now-pointless original re-render from renderResponse /
  renderPerformed.
- *Fill the column width*: added ~#notation svg / #responseNotation svg /
  #performedNotation svg { width:100% !important; height:auto !important }~ so the
  abcjs SVG fills the pane (height follows via viewBox). Canvas-size model:
  responsive:"resize" + svg{width:100%} → fills container width; %%staffwidth =
  internal layout density (LOWER staffwidth → more magnified/taller when filled).
- cache-bust app.js?v=5 + assessment.css?v=5.

** [2026-06-24] Notation: stable two-pane + single octave bracket
- *Half-size notation after recording*: the response was rendered while its pane
  was still collapsed (:has → display:none → 0 width), and the original kept its
  full-width viewBox after the row split. FIX (renderResponse / renderPerformed):
  show() the pane FIRST, then in requestAnimationFrame render the response (now
  measures its real half-pane width) and RE-RENDER the original #notation (re-fit to
  its new half-pane). Re-render only the original — a global window 'resize' would
  make abcjs re-render ALL tunes, wiping the response colors + octave bracket.
- *Single dotted 8vb bracket*: when every detected note shares the same nonzero
  octShift (whole pattern an octave away), drop the per-note "^8vb" marks; instead
  ~drawOctaveBracket()~ appends one italic label + dotted line (+ end hook) over the
  passage via SVG (getBBox of .abcjs-note). Mixed octaves still mark per note.
- Cache-busted app.js → ?v=4 (it changed; css already fresh after the user's
  hard-refresh). Browser-heuristic caching of python http.server static files.

** [2026-06-24] Assessment parity: original notation full-width until response
- All three pages share identical structure/CSS (verified: no dark remnants,
  column-screen, shared.css). The tonal/rhythm "step behind" look = the EMPTY right
  score-pane on load (tune has a single full-width notation). Fix: CSS :has() —
  while #responseCard/#performedCard carry .hidden, the .score-row collapses to one
  column and the empty pane is display:none → original notation is full-width like
  the tune; once the app reveals the card (show()=remove .hidden), it splits to two
  panes (original left, response right). Graceful fallback (stays 2-col) on browsers
  without :has().

** [2026-06-24] Assessment pages: full redesign (light, site-integrated)
- New shared ~apps/shared/assessment.css~ (light theme + layout) loaded by all three

** [2026-06-24] Assessment two-column layout + chord-sheet label stretch fix
- *Two columns on wide screens*: each assessment partial wraps its cards in
  ~<div class="cols"><div class="col">…</div><div class="col">…</div></div>~
  (left = setup/mic/live; right = result/recording/session/options — order matches
  document order so narrow screens stack identically). CSS (each app.css):
  @media(min-width:980px){ #app-root{max-width:1180px} .cols{display:flex;
  align-items:flex-start;gap:1.25rem} .col{flex:1 1 0;min-width:0} }. Used explicit
  flex columns (NOT multicol) so cards don't hop columns when result/recording
  cards grow during use.
- *Chord-sheet label stretch*: svgNoteLabel had textLength+lengthAdjust=
  'spacingAndGlyphs' → stretched glyph SHAPES of 2-char labels (B♭ widened the
  capital B). Removed both; kept letter-spacing -0.5 so the accidental tucks close.
  Capital letter now matches single-note label width. Both partial + source.

** [2026-06-24] Chord-sheet: note exclusion, label-only play, restyled labels
- *Play restricted to the chord LABEL*: svgDiagramTitle adds a transparent hit
  rect (+ class diagram-title) over the top 40px; card click plays only when
  e.target.closest('.diagram-title'). (Was whole-card.)
- *Click a note circle to include/exclude it from the voicing*: per-token,
  per-instrument ~t.excluded[inst.id]~ Set of pitch classes. Circles+labels tagged
  class=note-marker data-pc; card click toggles, redraw() (drawKbd/drawFret closure
  via a ~redraw~ var). Excluded → EXCL_COLORS grey; card._midis (the played voicing)
  filters out excluded pcs. PIANO DEFAULT: chord with >3 notes drops the 5th
  (pc=root+7). Fret has no default (starts empty).
- *Label restyle*: svgNoteLabel un-bolded (font-weight 500; root 700). labelForPc
  converts b/#/x → ♭/♯/♯♯. Root marker now dark fill (#2b2b2b) + WHITE bold text
  (was grey #c6c6c6 fill / dark text). Both partial + source edited (11 paired
  string replacements, node-checked).
- FUTURE (user): track per-student known inversions/voicings — each starts at root
  position only; default "favor voice leading" → TRUE and include only voicings the
  student has experience with (extensions will be tricky). Not built yet. See
  voice-leading note in prior entry.

** [2026-06-24] Chord-sheet: click-to-play, Sol→So, DO-based solfège
- *Click a chord to play its current voicing* (screen only; PDF unaffected — audio
  can't render to PDF, handlers no-op in print): buildDiagramCard stashes
  ~card._midis~ in drawKbd (buildChordMidis) and drawFret (pos string midis);
  card click → MusicAudio.chord(card._midis). Skips clicks on the voicing <select>.
  Edited BOTH partial + source (apps/chord-library/chord-sheet.html).
- *Sol → So*: only the chord-sheet SOLFEGE array had the "Sol" syllable. Verified
  the others already use it: solfege-keyboard solfegeList uses "So"; circle
  MODE_SOLFEGE uses "so". (assessment "sol" = a JS var; abcjs/director hits = the
  word/Solo, untouched.)
- *Solfège was resting-based (wrong for non-major)*: solfegeForPc used
  currentKeyContext().tonicPc (= manualKeyPc = RESTING tone), so it treated the
  resting tone as Do. Now DO-based: uses Tonality.getDo() (fallback to key context)
  → minor tonic = La. circle (per-mode) + solfege-keyboard (do-selector) already
  correct.

** [2026-06-24] Load-tune-chords: adopt tune tonality + key-aware chord spelling
- Tunes now also store ~tuneTonality~ {do,resting,mode} in publishTonality (next to
  tuneChords). loadTuneChords reads it → render()+publish() so DO/Tonality/Resting
  dropdowns (and shared tonality, sel-key) snap to the tune's key BEFORE loading.
- Chord-sheet ~bestSpelling~ was key-AGNOSTIC (lowest-accidental/canonical). Patched
  (partial + source) to be key-aware: when manualKeyPc set, spell on the line of
  fifths via window.PitchSpelling.spellPitchClass(rootPc, keyTonicLOF(manualKeyPc)).
  → B major chords show D♯/F♯, B♭ major B♭/E♭, C minor A♭/B♭ (verified). Applies to
  ALL chords, not just imported ones (consistent). Regenerated _tunes-embed.qmd.
- *FOLLOWUP (A# still showed)*: renderChips uses ~t.rootName~ (the LITERAL parsed
  token), only falling back to chordDisplayName/bestSpelling when rootName is empty
  — so textarea-parsed chords never hit the key-aware path. FIX: loadTuneChords now
  RE-SPELLS each chord root (parseEntry → spellAtLof centered on resting LOF, ASCII)
  before filling the textarea, so the token itself is correct ("A#"→"Bb" in flat
  keys; stays "A#" in B major). Verified.

** [2026-06-24] Tune chord import = unique chords only
- publishChords dedupe changed consecutive→UNIQUE (names.indexOf===-1), so each
  chord lists once, first-seen order (Lightly Row → "Bb F7"). Regenerated
  practice/_tunes-embed.qmd ([[deiframe-partials-stale]]).
- FUTURE (user flagged): voice leading will want *contextual* voicings — the same
  chord may need different voicings depending on neighbors. Current import is just
  the chord-symbol set; revisit when adding progression-aware voicing.

** [2026-06-24] Chord-sheet combobox spelling + stale-partial fix
- DO/Resting are now editable *comboboxes* (input + shared datalist), not selects:
  type with b/#/bb/##/x (or pick a suggestion); display uses ♯/♭ glyphs. Full
  enharmonic range incl. Cb/B#/Fb/E# and double accidentals.
- Spelling is line-of-fifths centered on the ANCHOR (the field last edited), via
  parseEntry → {pc, lof}; the other field = spellPitchClass(otherPc, anchorLof).
  Fixes: DO=B minor → resting G♯ (was Ab); resting=Ab minor → DO C♭ (was B);
  resting=C minor → DO E♭. Earlier bug was spelling resting centered on itself +
  Cb/B# missing from the fixed option list (fell back to wrong enharmonic).
  applyFromShared anchors on resting with keyTonicLOF default (pc-only storage
  can't distinguish G#m vs Abm — defaults flat; user can retype).
- *Load-tune-chords "No tune chords"* root cause: the de-iframed
  practice/_tunes-embed.qmd was generated BEFORE publishChords was added (stale
  static copy). FIX: regenerated _tunes/_patterns/_rhythms-embed.qmd from current
  sources. LESSON: re-running the de-iframe transform is required after editing the
  source apps/*/index.html — the partials don't auto-update.

- *Enharmonic DO/resting dropdowns*: options now list every spelling

** [2026-06-24] Chord-sheet: 3 key dropdowns, fix load btn, piano default, persist
- The chord-sheet tool is an *IIFE* — ALL integration must go through the DOM
  (#chords-textarea parsed on blur, #sel-key change handler, [data-inst-id] boxes).
  That's why the first "Load tune chords" (calling appendToken) did nothing.
- *Three key dropdowns* replace the single "X major" #sel-key (now hidden, still
  the tool's internal key input): DO (pitch), Tonality (Major/Minor/Dorian/…),
  Resting tone (pitch). Relation: do = (resting + MO[mode]*7) mod 12; resting =
  (do − MO[mode]*7) mod 12. Change DO→recompute resting; change resting→recompute
  DO; change tonality→keep resting, recompute DO. Each change publishes
  Tonality.setAll(do,resting,mode) AND drives #sel-key=resting so romans use the
  resting tonic. Pitch labels re-spell via spellPc on DO change.
- *Load tune chords* fixed: sets #chords-textarea = tuneChords.join + dispatches
  'blur' → tool's parseTextarea. Feedback if empty ("open a tune first").
- *Default piano*: S.instruments init 'guitar'→'keyboard' (both partial + source).
- *Remember instruments*: localStorage "csInstruments" = {enabled ids, hand-mode,
  voice-leading, fingerings}; applied on load (retries until #instrument-list
  built), saved on any change.

** [2026-06-24] Chord-sheet: autopopulate from tune + DO/resting awareness
- *Tunes publishes chords*: ~publishChords()~ (in setTune) extracts the tune's
  chord symbols, transposes by vis-transpose, spells via Tonality.spellPc, dedupes
  consecutive → localStorage "tuneChords" (e.g. Lightly Row → Bb F7 Bb F7…).
- *Chord-sheet builder* (tools/_chord-sheet.qmd):
  - Roman key context now = RESTING TONE: ~#sel-key~ (values are pitch classes →
    manualKeyPc → currentKeyContext().tonicPc) is set from Tonality.getResting().
    (Was bound to DO — wrong for minor romans.) Note: sel-key labels still say
    "X major" (cosmetic); tonic is correct so romans work.
  - "↪ Load tune chords" button (next to the roman Add) → ~loadTuneChords()~:
    clears the sheet + ~addChordByName()~ each (parses root+quality → chordId →
    the sheet's ~appendToken~). Reuses chord-sheet globals (appendToken, parseNote,
    noteName, preferFlats, parsedTokens) by name (inlined → same scope).
- Caveat: button replaces the current sheet (explicit load, not auto-on-every-tune
  to avoid clobbering edits).

** [2026-06-24] Tunes memory; patterns minor-switch; circle chords; shared audio
- *Tunes remembers last*: ~saveTunesState()~ (in setTune) persists {collection,

- *Circle minor default → Harmonic Minor* (C_MODE: m/min/minor→6; aeo/aeolian→5 so

- *Direct key selection* (instead of half-steps): added a "Key" dropdown to the

  - patterns (written tonic C): consumes resting → vis-transpose = resting pc;
    local picks just transpose (consumer-only, doesn't publish).
  - tunes (master): ~onKeySelect~ transposes so resting tone = chosen pitch

- Onset resolution is limited by rAF frame rate (~16ms); fast microbeats at high

* Landscape Research (2026-06-22)

** Top-line finding
There is *no single open-source project* that does the whole pipeline
(assessment delivery → recording → auto-grading → manual-review storage).
The academic systems that do exactly this are *research codebases*, not
deployable platforms — useful as references/algorithms, not as a drop-in
backend. Practical path: browser library for capture + pitch/onset detection,
then ~100 lines of our own grading logic (passages are short → easy + full
control).

** Layer 1 — Capture in the browser (MIDI + audio)
- *WEBMIDI.js* / *webmidi* (https://webmidijs.org) and *JZZ* (https://jzz.js.org)
  — wrap the raw Web MIDI API. For MIDI-entry tasks this is all we need: note-on
  events with timestamps = the data we grade on. MIDI is the *easiest* case:
  perfect onset times + pitches handed to us, no detection.
- *Web Audio API* ~MediaRecorder~ — record mic to a .webm/.wav blob for the
  manual-review archive (Layer 4). Capture raw regardless of grading.

** Layer 2 — Pitch & onset detection (sung/played audio)
- *Pitchfinder* (JS, npm) https://github.com/peterkhayes/pitchfinder — multiple
  algorithms (YIN, AMDF, ...) on Float32Arrays. Best-maintained JS option; YIN
  is right for monophonic voice/instrument.
- *cwilso/PitchDetect* https://github.com/cwilso/PitchDetect — canonical
  autocorrelation demo; good to read, simpler than we'll ultimately want.
- *aubio* (C, + Python/WASM) https://github.com/aubio/aubio — the serious
  option. Onset detection (attack segmentation) + pitch + audio→MIDI. For robust
  rhythm grading from audio, aubio's onset detector is the standard tool.
  Compile to WASM for browser, or run server-side on the uploaded recording.

Mapping tasks → detection:
- *Rhythm-blind tonal accuracy* → need pitch only (median F0 per note).
  Pitchfinder/YIN is enough.
- *Pitch-blind rhythmic accuracy* → need onset times only. aubio onset
  detection cleaner than deriving from pitch tracks.

** Layer 3 — Grading logic
Recurring technique across the literature: *Dynamic Time Warping (DTW)* to align
student note sequence to reference, then measure deviation. For 1–5 note
passages we may not even need DTW — direct positional comparison works:
- *Tonal*: deviation of each note from target in *cents*; threshold (e.g.
  ±50 cents = correct).
- *Rhythmic*: normalize onset times to tempo (or to total duration → tempo-blind),
  compare inter-onset intervals vs target ratios with a tolerance window.

Reference implementations to mine for algorithms:
- *helenacuesta/intonation-assessment* (Python)
  https://github.com/helenacuesta/intonation-assessment — per-note intonation
  scoring (TROMPA/Cantamus choir project); color-coded per-note accuracy.
  Closest to "production-minded" of the research repos.
- *madhavlab/2023_narottam_engine* — singing-mistake detection; dataset
  annotated for pitch, amplitude, pronunciation *and rhythm* errors; rule-based +
  DL (CNN/CRNN/TCN).
- *MTG/smc-2016* https://github.com/MTG/smc-2016 — teacher-vs-student intonation
  comparison (F0 extraction, transcription, alignment); clean
  compare-to-reference template.
- *Matchmaker* (Python) https://arxiv.org/html/2510.10087v1 — real-time score
  following; overkill for 5 notes but relevant if passages grow.

** Layer 4 — Saving recordings for manual review
No special project needed: ~MediaRecorder~ blob → upload to storage (S3/etc.),
store alongside the auto-grade and the detected note/onset data so a reviewer
sees both audio + what the grader "heard". Keeping the extracted F0/onset trace
next to the audio makes manual review much faster.

** Suggested assembly
1. *MIDI tasks*: WEBMIDI.js → comparison logic. (Trivial; do first.)
2. *Audio tasks*: Web Audio capture → Pitchfinder (pitch) and/or aubio-wasm
   (onset) → DTW/positional comparison → store blob + grade.
3. Steal scoring thresholds from intonation-assessment / narottam repos rather
   than reinventing tolerance tuning.

** Key open design decision
*Run detection client-side or server-side?*
- Client-side (WASM): instant feedback.
- Server-side (Python aubio + research code): more robust algorithms; keeps
  grading logic out of the user's hands — matters for high-stakes grades.

** Sources
- https://github.com/peterkhayes/pitchfinder
- https://github.com/cwilso/PitchDetect
- https://github.com/notthetup/awesome-webaudio

* Decisions
- [2026-06-22] First demo target = *rhythm-blind tonal accuracy on a single
  sustained pitch*. Rationale: monophonic pitch detection of a held note is the
  single most robust thing in the whole space → highest chance of immediate
  success. MIDI deferred per user.
- [2026-06-22] Demo is *fully client-side, zero-dependency, single HTML file*
  using autocorrelation (ACF2+). Defers the client/server question; good enough
  to validate UX + detection quality.

* Work Log
** [2026-06-22] Session 1
- Did landscape research (above).
- Created this running notes file.
- Built first audio demo: ~demos/tonal-accuracy.html~ (see Demos section).
  - Live tuner readout (frequency, nearest note, cents).
  - Pick a target note; "Record attempt" captures ~1.5s, grades median F0
    against target in cents (±50¢ default = pass).
  - Zero deps; run via local http server (getUserMedia needs secure context).
- Extended to 3-note passages: ~demos/tonal-passage-3.html~.
  - Three dropdowns for the target passage (defaults C4–E4–G4 arpeggio).
  - *Segmentation strategy*: since the task is rhythm-blind, the student sings
    the 3 notes with a short silent gap between each; the grader splits the
    recording on silence (~GAP_MS=120ms~ of unvoiced frames ends a note,
    ~MIN_VOICED=5~ frames = a real note), then grades each segment's
    middle-60% median pitch in cents, in performance order.
  - Per-note results table + overall PASS/FAIL. Requires exactly 3 detected
    segments, else it tells the user to add clearer gaps.
  - "Hear passage" plays the 3 targets as separate tones (note+gap) to model
    how to perform it.

** [2026-06-22] Session 1 — detector fix
- Symptom: demo "picking up a lot of noise" + accurate performances FAILING.
- Diagnosis:
  1. Old detector (ACF2+ autocorrelation) gated only on volume (RMS>0.01) →
     any background sound returned a bogus pitch → spurious voiced frames broke
     the silence-based segmentation.
  2. Raw autocorrelation is octave-error prone (locks to sub-harmonic). The live
     meter snaps to the *nearest* note so it still looked in tune, but grading is
     vs a *specific target octave* → octave error reads as ~1200¢ off → FAIL on a
     correctly sung note.
- Fix: replaced detector with *YIN* in BOTH demos.
  - YIN's cumulative-mean-normalized difference + absolute threshold = octave
    robust; its aperiodicity gives a real confidence so noise/unvoiced returns
    -1 instead of a guess (this is the noise gate).
  - Params: ~RMS_GATE=0.012~, ~YIN_THRESHOLD=0.15~, ~FMIN=70~, ~FMAX=1100~.
  - Added per-note octave-outlier rejection in the 3-note grader (drop frames
    >150¢ from the provisional median before taking the final median).
- Tuning knobs if issues persist: raise YIN_THRESHOLD (more permissive / catches
  breathy voices) or RMS_GATE (more aggressive silence gating in a loud room).

** [2026-06-22] Session 1 — short capture + octave tolerance
- Added 2s and 3s options to the 3-note demo capture dropdown (for quick
  testing of the detector/octave changes). Default still 5s.
- Added *octave-tolerance toggle* (~#octtol~, default ON): grading picks the
  best-fitting octave among target ±0/±1/±2 octaves via ~bestCents()~. Helps
  with (a) students singing in a different octave and (b) any residual octave
  detection errors. Per-note row shows "(+1 oct)" etc. when a transposition was
  applied; result footer notes whether octave tolerance was on.
- (Only applied to ~tonal-passage-3.html~ so far; can mirror to the single-note
  demo if needed.)

** [2026-06-22] Session 1 — needle damping
- Live tuner was twitchy (YIN frame jitter + needle snapping instantly).
- Added *display-only* exponential smoothing (EMA) on frequency in both demos:
  needle/readout now driven by ~smoothedFreq~ for physical-needle inertia.
  - ~NEEDLE_ALPHA=0.15~ (lower = heavier/slower needle).
  - Snaps instead of gliding when the pitch jumps >200¢ (new note) so it stays
    responsive; holds last reading on unvoiced frames (no flicker).
  - *Grading is unaffected*: raw per-frame pitches still feed ~collected~ /
    ~samples~; only the display is smoothed. CSS untouched.

** [2026-06-22] Session 1 — recorder wired up (Layer 4)
- Added ~MediaRecorder~ capture to ~tonal-passage-3.html~. During the grading
  window it records the mic to a Blob; afterward shows an ~<audio>~ player +
  a download link.
  - MIME picked by feature detection: opus/webm → webm → mp4 → ogg; graceful
    no-op if MediaRecorder unsupported (Safari quirks).
  - Filename: ~attempt_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS_<pass|fail|incomplete>.<ext>~ — the grade
    is embedded so manual-review files self-label.
  - Record button disabled during capture; object URLs revoked between attempts.
- *Demo persistence = local download for now.* Real app: POST the Blob + the
  grade JSON + the detected F0/segment trace to object storage (S3) so a
  reviewer sees audio + what the grader "heard" (see Layer 4 plan).

* Demos
** demos/tonal-accuracy.html
Single-pitch tonal-accuracy grader. Zero dependencies.

Run:
#+begin_src sh
cd /Users/sean/Projects/record-and-grade
python3 -m http.server 8000
# then open http://localhost:8000/demos/tonal-accuracy.html
#+end_src

Why localhost and not just opening the file: ~getUserMedia~ (mic access)
requires a *secure context*; ~http://localhost~ qualifies, ~file://~ generally
does not.

How grading works:
- Autocorrelation (ACF2+) estimates fundamental frequency every animation frame.
- On "Record attempt", it collects per-frame F0 for ~1.5s, takes the *median*
  (robust to onset/offset glitches), converts to cents relative to the target,
  pass if |cents| ≤ tolerance.
